t_id.kemenag: Ia (jibril) berkata: "Sesungguhnya aku ini hanyalah seorang utusan Tuhanmu, untuk memberimu seorang anak laki-laki yang suci".
t_id.sabiq: Malaikat Jibrîl berkata, "Aku hanyalah seorang utusan dari Tuhanmu, untuk menjadi perantara dalam pemberian seorang anak laki-laki yang suci dan baik kepadamu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ia berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku ini adalah utusan Rabbmu, untuk memberimu seorang anak laki-laki yang suci)" yang kelak menjadi nabi.
t_id.iwq: Ia (jibril) berkata: "Sesungguhnya aku ini hanyalah seorang utusan Tuhanmu, untuk memberimu seorang anak laki-laki yang suci".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال إنما أنا رسول ربك ليهب لك غلاما زكيا» بالنبوة
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ إِنَّمَآ أَنَا۠ رَسُولُ رَبِّكِ لِأَهَبَ لَكِ غُلَٰمٗا زَكِيّٗا (زَكِيًّا: طَاهِرًا مِنَ الذُّنُوبِ مُبَارَكًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ia berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku ini adalah utusan Rabbmu, untuk memberimu seorang anak laki-laki yang suci)" yang kelak menjadi nabi

ar: قَالَتۡ أَنَّىٰ یَكُونُ لِی غُلَـٰمࣱ وَلَمۡ یَمۡسَسۡنِی بَشَرࣱ وَلَمۡ أَكُ بَغِیࣰّا
ar_simple: قَالَتْ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَامٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِي بَشَرٌ وَلَمْ أَكُ بَغِيًّا
transliteration: qālat annā yakūnu lī ghulāmun walam yamsasnī basharun walam aku baghiyyan

t.sahih-int: She said, "How can I have a boy while no man has touched me and I have not been unchaste
t.asad: Said she: "How can I have a son when no man has ever touched me? - for, never have I been a loose woman
t.yusuf-ali: She said: "How shall I have a son, seeing that no man has touched me, and I am not unchaste
t.pickthall: She said: How can I have a son when no mortal hath touched me, neither have I been unchaste
t.arberry: She said, 'How shall I have a son whom no mortal has touched, neither have I been unchaste
t.hilali-khan: She said: "How can I have a son, when no man has touched me, nor am I unchaste
t.itani: She said, “How can I have a son, when no man has touched me, and I was never unchaste?”
t.mubarakpuri: She said: "How can I have a son when no man has touched me, nor am I Baghiyya"
t.qarib: She said, "How can I have a son when no man has touched me and I have not been a promiscuous woman
t.wahiduddin: She said, "How can I have a son when no man has touched me; and neither have I been unchaste
t.shakir: She said: When shall I have a boy and no mortal has yet touched me, nor have I been unchaste
t.haleem: She said, ‘How can I have a son when no man has touched me? I have not been unchaste,’
t.maariful: She said, “How shall I have a boy while no human has ever touched me, nor have I ever been unchaste?”
t.khattab: She wondered, “How can I have a son when no man has ever touched me, nor am I unchaste?”
t.ghali: She said, "However can I have a youth, and no mortal has touched me, neither have I been a prostitute
t.irving: She said: "How shall I have a boy when no human being has ever touched me, nor am I a loose woman
t.iwq: She said, "How can I have a boy while no man has touched me and I have not been unchaste

t_id.kemenag: Maryam berkata: "Bagaimana akan ada bagiku seorang anak laki-laki, sedang tidak pernah seorang manusiapun menyentuhku dan aku bukan (pula) seorang pezina!"
t_id.sabiq: Maryam berkata, "Bagaimana aku dapat mempunyai anak, sedang aku tidak pernah disentuh oleh seseorang pun, dan aku bukan seorang pezina."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maryam berkata, "Bagaimana akan ada bagiku seorang anak laki-laki, sedangkan tidak pernah seorang manusia pun menyentuhku) yakni mengawiniku (dan aku bukan pula seorang pezina!)" seorang pelacur.
t_id.iwq: Maryam berkata: "Bagaimana akan ada bagiku seorang anak laki-laki, sedang tidak pernah seorang manusiapun menyentuhku dan aku bukan (pula) seorang pezina!"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قالت أنَّى يكون لي غلام ولم يمسسني بشر» يتزوج «ولم أك بغيّا» زانية
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَتۡ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَٰمٞ وَلَمۡ يَمۡسَسۡنِي بَشَرٞ وَلَمۡ أَكُ بَغِيّٗا (بَغِيًّا: زَانِيَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maryam berkata, "Bagaimana akan ada bagiku seorang anak laki-laki, sedangkan tidak pernah seorang manusia pun menyentuhku) yakni mengawiniku (dan aku bukan pula seorang pezina!)" seorang pelacur

ar: قَالَ كَذَ ٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ هُوَ عَلَیَّ هَیِّنࣱۖ وَلِنَجۡعَلَهُۥۤ ءَایَةࣰ لِّلنَّاسِ وَرَحۡمَةࣰ مِّنَّاۚ وَكَانَ أَمۡرࣰا مَّقۡضِیࣰّا
ar_simple: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٌ ۖ وَلِنَجْعَلَهُ آيَةً لِلنَّاسِ وَرَحْمَةً مِنَّا ۚ وَكَانَ أَمْرًا مَقْضِيًّا
transliteration: qāla kadhāliki qāla rabbuki huwa ʿalayya hayyinun walinajʿalahu āyatan lilnnāsi waraḥmatan minnā wakāna amran maqḍiyyan

t.sahih-int: He said, "Thus [it will be]; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign to the people and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter [already] decreed
t.asad: [The angel] answered: "Thus it is; [but] thy Sustainer says, `This is easy for Me; and [thou shalt have a son,] so that We might make him a symbol unto mankind and an act of grace from US. And it was a thing decreed [by God]
t.yusuf-ali: He said: "So (it will be): Thy Lord saith, 'that is easy for Me: and (We wish) to appoint him as a Sign unto men and a Mercy from Us': It is a matter (so) decreed
t.pickthall: He said: So (it will be). Thy Lord saith: It is easy for Me. And (it will be) that We may make of him a revelation for mankind and a mercy from Us, and it is a thing ordained
t.arberry: He said, 'Even so thy Lord has said: "Easy is that for Me; and that We may appoint him a sign unto men and a mercy from Us; it is a thing decreed
t.hilali-khan: He said: "So (it will be), your Lord said: 'That is easy for Me (Allah): And (We wish) to appoint him as a sign to mankind and a mercy from Us (Allah), and it is a matter (already) decreed, (by Allah). 
t.itani: He said, “Thus said your Lord, `It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign for humanity, and a mercy from Us. It is a matter already decided.'“
t.mubarakpuri: He said: "Thus says your Lord: `That is easy for Me (Allah). And (We wish) to appoint him as a sign to mankind and a mercy from Us (Allah), and it is a matter (already) decreed (by Allah)."'
t.qarib: He said, "It will be so. Your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign to people and a mercy from Us. The matter is already determined
t.wahiduddin: [The angel] replied, "So shall it be; your Lord says, 'This is easy for Me; and We shall make him a sign to people and a blessing, from Us. This has been decreed
t.shakir: He said: Even so; your Lord says: It is easy to Me: and that We may make him a sign to men and a mercy from Us, and it is a matter which has been decreed
t.haleem: and he said, ‘This is what your Lord said: “It is easy for Me- We shall make him a sign to all people, a blessing from Us.”’
t.maariful: He said, “So it is; your Lord said, ‘It is easy for Me, and (We will do this) so that We make it a sign for people and a mercy from Us, and this is a matter already destined.’”
t.khattab: He replied, “So will it be! Your Lord says, ‘It is easy for Me. And so will We make him a sign for humanity and a mercy from Us.’ It is a matter ˹already˺ decreed.”
t.ghali: He said, "Thus (it will be). Your Lord has said, cSimple is it for Me, and that We may make him a sign for mankind and a mercy from Us, and it is a Command decreed
t.irving: He said: "Thus your Lord has said: "It is a simple matter for Me [to do]. We will grant him as a sign for mankind and a mercy from Ourself." It is a matter that has been decided
t.iwq: He said, "Thus it will be; your Lord says, 'It is easy for Me, and We will make him a sign to the people and a mercy from Us. And it is a matter already decreed

t_id.kemenag: Jibril berkata: "Demikianlah". Tuhanmu berfirman: "Hal itu adalah mudah bagi-Ku; dan agar dapat Kami menjadikannya suatu tanda bagi manusia dan sebagai rahmat dari Kami; dan hal itu adalah suatu perkara yang sudah diputuskan".
t_id.sabiq: Jibrîl berkata, "Benar apa yang kamu katakan. Kamu memang tidak pernah disentuh seorang pun. Kendatipun demikian, Tuhanmu telah berfirman, 'Pemberian anak tanpa seorang bapak adalah soal yang mudah bagi-Ku, agar menjadi bukti bagi manusia akan besarnya kekuasaan Kami, dan untuk menjadi rahmat bagi orang yang menjadikannya sebagai petunjuk. Sesungguhnya penciptaan 'Isâ adalah sesuatu yang telah ditetapkan, dan pasti terjadi'."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Jibril berkata,) perkaranya memang ("Demikianlah.") yaitu akan diciptakan bagimu seorang anak laki-laki tanpa ayah (Rabbmu berfirman, "Hal itu adalah mudah bagi-Ku) yaitu dengan cara Aku memerintahkan kepada malaikat Jibril supaya meniup dirimu, lalu karena itu kamu mengandung. Mengingat kalimat yang telah disebutkan mengandung makna illat atau kausalita, maka kalimat berikutnya di'athafkan kepadanya, yaitu (dan agar dapat Kami menjadikannya suatu tanda bagi manusia) yang menunjukkan akan kekuasaan-Ku (dan sebagai rahmat dari Kami) bagi orang-orang yang beriman kepadanya (dan hal itu adalah) penciptaan itu merupakan (suatu perkara yang sudah diputuskan)" di dalam ilmu-Ku, malaikat Jibril meniupkan nafasnya ke dalam baju kurung Maryam, seketika itu juga Maryam merasakan di dalam kandungannya terdapat seorang bayi.
t_id.iwq: Jibril berkata: "Demikianlah". Tuhanmu berfirman: "Hal itu adalah mudah bagi-Ku; dan agar dapat Kami menjadikannya suatu tanda bagi manusia dan sebagai rahmat dari Kami; dan hal itu adalah suatu perkara yang sudah diputuskan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال» الأمر «كذلك» من خلق غلام منك من غير أب «قال ربك هو عليَّ هينٌ» أي: بأن ينفخ بأمري جبريل فيك فتحملي به ولكون ما ذكر في معنى العلة عطف عليه «ولنجعله آيةً للناس» على قدرتنا «ورحمة منا» لمن آمن به «وكان» خلقه «أمرا مقضيا» به في علمي فنفخ جبريل في جيب درعها فأحست بالحمل في بطنها مصورا
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٞۖ وَلِنَجۡعَلَهُۥٓ ءَايَةٗ لِّلنَّاسِ وَرَحۡمَةٗ مِّنَّاۚ وَكَانَ أَمۡرٗا مَّقۡضِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Jibril berkata,) perkaranya memang ("Demikianlah.") yaitu akan diciptakan bagimu seorang anak laki-laki tanpa ayah (Rabbmu berfirman, "Hal itu adalah mudah bagi-Ku) yaitu dengan cara Aku memerintahkan kepada malaikat Jibril supaya meniup dirimu, lalu karena itu kamu mengandung. Mengingat kalimat yang telah disebutkan mengandung makna illat atau kausalita, maka kalimat berikutnya di'athafkan kepadanya, yaitu (dan agar dapat Kami menjadikannya suatu tanda bagi manusia) yang menunjukkan akan kekuasaan-Ku (dan sebagai rahmat dari Kami) bagi orang-orang yang beriman kepadanya (dan hal itu adalah) penciptaan itu merupakan (suatu perkara yang sudah diputuskan)" di dalam ilmu-Ku, malaikat Jibril meniupkan nafasnya ke dalam baju kurung Maryam, seketika itu juga Maryam merasakan di dalam kandungannya terdapat seorang bayi

ar: ۞ فَحَمَلَتۡهُ فَٱنتَبَذَتۡ بِهِۦ مَكَانࣰا قَصِیࣰّا
ar_simple: ۞ فَحَمَلَتْهُ فَانْتَبَذَتْ بِهِ مَكَانًا قَصِيًّا
transliteration: faḥamalathu fa-intabadhat bihi makānan qaṣiyyan

t.sahih-int: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place
t.asad: and in time she conceived him, and then she withdrew with him to a far-off place
t.yusuf-ali: So she conceived him, and she retired with him to a remote place
t.pickthall: And she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a far place
t.arberry: So she conceived him, and withdrew with him to a distant place
t.hilali-khan: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a far place (i.e. Bethlehem valley about 4-6 miles from Jerusalem)
t.itani: So she carried him, and secluded herself with him in a remote place.
t.mubarakpuri: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place.
t.qarib: So she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place
t.wahiduddin: So she conceived him and withdrew with him to a distant place
t.shakir: So she conceived him; then withdrew herself with him to a remote place
t.haleem: And so it was ordained: she conceived him. She withdrew to a distant place
t.maariful: So she conceived him, and went in seclusion with him to a remote place
t.khattab: So she conceived him and withdrew with him to a remote place
t.ghali: So she conceived him, then she retired with him to a remote place
t.irving: So she conceived him, and withdrew to a remote place to have him
t.iwq: Thus she conceived him, and she withdrew with him to a remote place

t_id.kemenag: Maka Maryam mengandungnya, lalu ia menyisihkan diri dengan kandungannya itu ke tempat yang jauh.
t_id.sabiq: Kehendak Allah pun terwujud. Maryam pun mengandung 'Isâ a. s. sesuai dengan kehendak-Nya. Maryam, dengan kandungan itu, menjauhkan diri dari manusia di tempat yang jauh.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Maryam mengandungnya, lalu ia mengasingkan diri) menjauhkan diri (dengan membawa kandungannya ke tempat yang jauh) jauh dari keluarganya.
t_id.iwq: Maka Maryam mengandungnya, lalu ia menyisihkan diri dengan kandungannya itu ke tempat yang jauh.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فحملته فانتبذت» تنحَّت «به مكانا قصيا» بعيدا من أهلها
tafsir.siraj-ar: ۞فَحَمَلَتۡهُ فَٱنتَبَذَتۡ بِهِۦ مَكَانٗا قَصِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Maryam mengandungnya, lalu ia mengasingkan diri) menjauhkan diri (dengan membawa kandungannya ke tempat yang jauh) jauh dari keluarganya

ar: فَأَجَاۤءَهَا ٱلۡمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذۡعِ ٱلنَّخۡلَةِ قَالَتۡ یَـٰلَیۡتَنِی مِتُّ قَبۡلَ هَـٰذَا وَكُنتُ نَسۡیࣰا مَّنسِیࣰّا
ar_simple: فَأَجَاءَهَا الْمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ قَالَتْ يَا لَيْتَنِي مِتُّ قَبْلَ هَٰذَا وَكُنْتُ نَسْيًا مَنْسِيًّا
transliteration: fa-ajāahā l-makhāḍu ilā jidh'ʿi l-nakhlati qālat yālaytanī mittu qabla hādhā wakuntu nasyan mansiyyan

t.sahih-int: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten
t.asad: And [when] the throes of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm-tree, she exclaimed: "Oh, would that I had died ere this, and had become a thing forgotten, utterly forgotten
t.yusuf-ali: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm-tree: She cried (in her anguish): "Ah! would that I had died before this! would that I had been a thing forgotten and out of sight
t.pickthall: And the pangs of childbirth drove her unto the trunk of the palm-tree. She said: Oh, would that I had died ere this and had become a thing of naught, forgotten
t.arberry: And the birthpangs surprised her by the trunk of the palm-tree. She said, 'Would I had died ere this, and become a thing forgotten
t.hilali-khan: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a date-palm. She said: "Would that I had died before this, and had been forgotten and out of sight
t.itani: The labor-pains came upon her, by the trunk of a palm-tree. She said, “I wish I had died before this, and been completely forgotten.”
t.mubarakpuri: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a date palm. She said: "Would that I had died before this, and had been forgotten and out of sight!"
t.qarib: Then the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had been dead and forgotten before all this
t.wahiduddin: The pains of labour drove her to the trunk of a date-palm. She said, "Oh, if only I had died before this and passed into oblivion
t.shakir: And the throes (of childbirth) compelled her to betake herself to the trunk of a palm tree. She said: Oh, would that I had died before this, and had been a thing quite forgotten
t.haleem: and, when the pains of childbirth drove her to [cling to] the trunk of a palm tree, she exclaimed, ‘I wish I had been dead and forgotten long before all this!’
t.maariful: Then the labour pains brought her to the trunk of a palm-tree. She said “O that I would have died before this, and would have been something gone, forgotten.”
t.khattab: Then the pains of labour drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She cried, “Alas! I wish I had died before this, and was a thing long forgotten!”
t.ghali: Then the birthpangs made her come to the trunk of the palm-tree. She said, "Oh, would I had died before this and become a thing forgotten, completely forgotten
t.irving: Labor pains came over her by the trunk of a datepalm. She said: "If only I had died before this, and been forgotten, overlooked
t.iwq: And the pains of childbirth drove her to the trunk of a palm tree. She said, "Oh, I wish I had died before this and was in oblivion, forgotten

t_id.kemenag: Maka rasa sakit akan melahirkan anak memaksa ia (bersandar) pada pangkal pohon kurma, dia berkata: "Aduhai, alangkah baiknya aku mati sebelum ini, dan aku menjadi barang yang tidak berarti, lagi dilupakan".
t_id.sabiq: Rasa sakit hendak melahirkan memaksa Maryam untuk bersandar dan menutup dirinya pada pangkal pohon kurma. Ia membayangkan kemungkinan sikap ingkar keluarganya terhadap kelahiran anaknya kelak. Ia pun berharap cepat meninggal dunia supaya kejadian ini tidak lagi berarti dan cepat dilupakan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka sewaktu datang kepadanya) ketika ia mengalami (rasa sakit akan melahirkan) yaitu rasa mulas karena akan melahirkan (-terpaksa ia bersandar- pada pangkal pohon kurma) yakni menyandarkan diri padanya, lalu ia melahirkan. Perlu diketahui bahwa sejak peniupan malaikat Jibril hingga melahirkan hanya memakan waktu sesaat saja (dia berkata, "Aduhai alangkah baiknya aku) lafal Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Tanbih atau ungkapan kekecewaan (mati sebelum ini) yakni sebelum perkara ini (dan aku menjadi barang yang tidak berarti, lagi dilupakan)" sebagai sesuatu yang tiada artinya, tidak dikenal dan tidak disebut-sebut.
t_id.iwq: Maka rasa sakit akan melahirkan anak memaksa ia (bersandar) pada pangkal pohon kurma, dia berkata: "Aduhai, alangkah baiknya aku mati sebelum ini, dan aku menjadi barang yang tidak berarti, lagi dilupakan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأجاءَها» جاءَ بها «المخاض» وجع الولادة «إلى جذع النخلة» لتعتمد عليه فولدت والحمل والتصوير والولادة في ساعة «قالت يا» للتنبيه «ليتني متُّ قبل هذا» الأمر «وكنت نسيا منسيا» شيئا متروكا لا يعرف ولا يذكر
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَجَآءَهَا ٱلۡمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذۡعِ ٱلنَّخۡلَةِ قَالَتۡ يَٰلَيۡتَنِي مِتُّ قَبۡلَ هَٰذَا وَكُنتُ نَسۡيٗا مَّنسِيّٗا (فَأَجَاءَهَا: فَأَلْجَأَهَا طَلْقُ الحَمْلِ, الْمَخَاضُ: الحَمْلُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka sewaktu datang kepadanya) ketika ia mengalami (rasa sakit akan melahirkan) yaitu rasa mulas karena akan melahirkan (-terpaksa ia bersandar- pada pangkal pohon kurma) yakni menyandarkan diri padanya, lalu ia melahirkan. Perlu diketahui bahwa sejak peniupan malaikat Jibril hingga melahirkan hanya memakan waktu sesaat saja (dia berkata, "Aduhai alangkah baiknya aku) lafal Ya di sini menunjukkan makna Tanbih atau ungkapan kekecewaan (mati sebelum ini) yakni sebelum perkara ini (dan aku menjadi barang yang tidak berarti, lagi dilupakan)" sebagai sesuatu yang tiada artinya, tidak dikenal dan tidak disebut-sebut

ar: فَنَادَىٰهَا مِن تَحۡتِهَاۤ أَلَّا تَحۡزَنِی قَدۡ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحۡتَكِ سَرِیࣰّا
ar_simple: فَنَادَاهَا مِنْ تَحْتِهَا أَلَّا تَحْزَنِي قَدْ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحْتَكِ سَرِيًّا
transliteration: fanādāhā min taḥtihā allā taḥzanī qad jaʿala rabbuki taḥtaki sariyyan

t.sahih-int: But he called her from below her, "Do not grieve; your Lord has provided beneath you a stream
t.asad: Thereupon [a voice] called out to her from beneath that [palm-tree]: "Grieve not! Thy Sustainer has provided a rivulet [running] beneath thee
t.yusuf-ali: But (a voice) cried to her from beneath the (palm-tree): "Grieve not! for thy Lord hath provided a rivulet beneath thee
t.pickthall: Then (one) cried unto her from below her, saying: Grieve not! Thy Lord hath placed a rivulet beneath thee
t.arberry: But the one that was below her called to her, 'Nay, do not sorrow; see, thy Lord has set below thee a rivulet
t.hilali-khan: Then [the babe 'Iesa (Jesus) or Jibrael (Gabriel)] cried unto her from below her, saying: "Grieve not! Your Lord has provided a water stream under you
t.itani: Whereupon he called her from beneath her: “Do not worry; your Lord has placed a stream beneath you.
t.mubarakpuri: Then he cried unto her from below her, saying: "Grieve not: your Lord has provided a Sariy under you."
t.qarib: But a voice called to her from below, "Do not grieve; your Lord has provided a stream beneath you
t.wahiduddin: But a voice called out to her from below, "Do not despair. Your Lord has provided a brook that runs at your feet
t.shakir: Then (the child) called out to her from beneath her: Grieve not, surely your Lord has made a stream to flow beneath you
t.haleem: but a voice cried to her from below, ‘Do not worry: your Lord has provided a stream at your feet
t.maariful: Then he called her from beneath her: “Do not grieve; your Lord has placed a stream beneath you
t.khattab: So a voice reassured her from below her, “Do not grieve! Your Lord has provided a stream at your feet
t.ghali: Then (one) called out to her from beneath her, "Do not grieve. Your Lord has already made beneath you a rivulet
t.irving: Someone called out to her from below where she was: "Don´t feel so sad! Your Lord has placed a brook at your feet
t.iwq: But he called her from below her, "Do not grieve; your Lord has provided beneath you a stream

t_id.kemenag: Maka Jibril menyerunya dari tempat yang rendah: "Janganlah kamu bersedih hati, sesungguhnya Tuhanmu telah menjadikan anak sungai di bawahmu.
t_id.sabiq: Maryam kemudian diseru oleh malaikat dari tempat yang rendah di bawahnya, "Janganlah kamu bersedih hati karena kesendirian, tidak ada makanan dan minuman serta karena gunjingan orang. Sesungguhnya Allah telah menjadikan anak sungai di dekatmu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Jibril menyerunya dari tempat yang lebih rendah,) pada saat itu malaikat Jibril berada di tempat yang lebih rendah dari tempat Maryam ("Janganlah kamu bersedih hati, sesungguhnya Rabbmu telah menjadikan anak sungai di bawahmu") yaitu sebuah sungai yang dahulunya kering kini berair kembali, berkat kekuasaan Allah.
t_id.iwq: Maka Jibril menyerunya dari tempat yang rendah: "Janganlah kamu bersedih hati, sesungguhnya Tuhanmu telah menjadikan anak sungai di bawahmu.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فناداها من تحتها» أي: جبريل وكان أسفل منها «ألا تحزني قد جعل ربك تحتك سريا» نهر ماء كان قد انقطع
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَنَادَىٰهَا مِن تَحۡتِهَآ أَلَّا تَحۡزَنِي قَدۡ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحۡتَكِ سَرِيّٗا (سَرِيًّا: جَدْوَلَ مَاءٍ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Jibril menyerunya dari tempat yang lebih rendah,) pada saat itu malaikat Jibril berada di tempat yang lebih rendah dari tempat Maryam ("Janganlah kamu bersedih hati, sesungguhnya Rabbmu telah menjadikan anak sungai di bawahmu") yaitu sebuah sungai yang dahulunya kering kini berair kembali, berkat kekuasaan Allah

ar: وَهُزِّیۤ إِلَیۡكِ بِجِذۡعِ ٱلنَّخۡلَةِ تُسَـٰقِطۡ عَلَیۡكِ رُطَبࣰا جَنِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ تُسَاقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَبًا جَنِيًّا
transliteration: wahuzzī ilayki bijidh'ʿi l-nakhlati tusāqiṭ ʿalayki ruṭaban janiyyan

t.sahih-int: And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree; it will drop upon you ripe, fresh dates
t.asad: and shake the trunk of the palm-tree towards thee: it will drop fresh, ripe dates upon thee
t.yusuf-ali: And shake towards thyself the trunk of the palm-tree: It will let fall fresh ripe dates upon thee
t.pickthall: And shake the trunk of the palm-tree toward thee, thou wilt cause ripe dates to fall upon thee
t.arberry: Shake also to thee the palm-trunk, and there shall come tumbling upon thee dates fresh and ripe
t.hilali-khan: And shake the trunk of date-palm towards you, it will let fall fresh ripe-dates upon you
t.itani: And shake the trunk of the palm-tree towards you, and it will drop ripe dates by you.”
t.mubarakpuri: "And shake the trunk of date palm towards you, it will let fall fresh ripe dates upon you."
t.qarib: Shake the trunk of the palm tree toward you; it will drop ripe, fresh dates to you
t.wahiduddin: and if you shake the trunk of this palm-tree, it will drop fresh ripe dates on you
t.shakir: And shake towards you the trunk of the palmtree, it will drop on you fresh ripe dates
t.haleem: and, if you shake the trunk of the palm tree towards you, it will deliver fresh ripe dates for you
t.maariful: Shake the trunk of the palm-tree towards yourself and, it will drop upon you ripe fresh dates
t.khattab: And shake the trunk of this palm tree towards you, it will drop fresh, ripe dates upon you
t.ghali: And shake to you the trunk of the palm-tree (and) it will let fall ripe dates down on you, readily reaped
t.irving: Shake the trunk of the datepalm towards you so it will drop some fresh dates on you
t.iwq: And shake toward you the trunk of the palm tree; it will drop upon you ripe, fresh dates

t_id.kemenag: Dan goyanglah pangkal pohon kurma itu ke arahmu, niscaya pohon itu akan menggugurkan buah kurma yang masak kepadamu,
t_id.sabiq: Dan gerakkan pangkal pohon kurma itu ke arahmu, ia akan menjatuhkan buahnya yang baik kepadamu(1). (1) Buah kurma yang matang mengandung zat makanan utama yang mudah dicerna. Karena itu, kurma menjadi makanan yang baik untuk wanita setelah melahirkan (masa nifas).
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan goyanglah pangkal pohon kurma itu ke arahmu) yang pada saat itu kering. Huruf Ba dalam lafal Bijidz'i adalah Zaidah atau tambahan (niscaya pohon itu akan menggugurkan) asal kata Tusaaqith adalah Tatasaaqath kemudian Ta yang kedua diganti menjadi Sin, selanjutnya diidgamkan pada Sin yang kedua. Menurut qiraat yang lain tetap dibaca seperti lafal asalnya (buah kurma kepadamu) lafal Ruthaban adalah Tamyiz (yang masak-masak) lafal Janiyyan menjadi sifat dari lafal Ruthaban.
t_id.iwq: Dan goyanglah pangkal pohon kurma itu ke arahmu, niscaya pohon itu akan menggugurkan buah kurma yang masak kepadamu,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وهزي إليك بجذع النخلة» كانت يابسة والباء زائدة «تساقط» أصله بتاءين قلبت الثانية سينا وأدغمت في السين، وفى قراءة تركها «عليك رطبا» تمييز «جنيا» صفته
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَهُزِّيٓ إِلَيۡكِ بِجِذۡعِ ٱلنَّخۡلَةِ تُسَٰقِطۡ عَلَيۡكِ رُطَبٗا جَنِيّٗا (جَنِيًّا: غَضًّا جُنِيَ مِنْ سَاعَتِهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan goyanglah pangkal pohon kurma itu ke arahmu) yang pada saat itu kering. Huruf Ba dalam lafal Bijidz'i adalah Zaidah atau tambahan (niscaya pohon itu akan menggugurkan) asal kata Tusaaqith adalah Tatasaaqath kemudian Ta yang kedua diganti menjadi Sin, selanjutnya diidgamkan pada Sin yang kedua. Menurut qiraat yang lain tetap dibaca seperti lafal asalnya (buah kurma kepadamu) lafal Ruthaban adalah Tamyiz (yang masak-masak) lafal Janiyyan menjadi sifat dari lafal Ruthaban

ar: فَكُلِی وَٱشۡرَبِی وَقَرِّی عَیۡنࣰاۖ فَإِمَّا تَرَیِنَّ مِنَ ٱلۡبَشَرِ أَحَدࣰا فَقُولِیۤ إِنِّی نَذَرۡتُ لِلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ صَوۡمࣰا فَلَنۡ أُكَلِّمَ ٱلۡیَوۡمَ إِنسِیࣰّا
ar_simple: فَكُلِي وَاشْرَبِي وَقَرِّي عَيْنًا ۖ فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ الْبَشَرِ أَحَدًا فَقُولِي إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ صَوْمًا فَلَنْ أُكَلِّمَ الْيَوْمَ إِنْسِيًّا
transliteration: fakulī wa-ish'rabī waqarrī ʿaynan fa-immā tarayinna mina l-bashari aḥadan faqūlī innī nadhartu lilrraḥmāni ṣawman falan ukallima l-yawma insiyyan

t.sahih-int: So eat and drink and be contented. And if you see from among humanity anyone, say, 'Indeed, I have vowed to the Most Merciful abstention, so I will not speak today to [any] man
t.asad: Eat, then, and drink, and let thine eye be gladdened! And if thou shouldst see any human being, convey this unto him: `Behold, abstinence from speech have I vowed unto the Most Gracious; hence, I may not speak today to any mortal
t.yusuf-ali: So eat and drink and cool (thine) eye. And if thou dost see any man, say, 'I have vowed a fast to (Allah) Most Gracious, and this day will I enter into not talk with any human being
t.pickthall: So eat and drink and be consoled. And if thou meetest any mortal, say: Lo! I have vowed a fast unto the Beneficent, and may not speak this day to any mortal
t.arberry: Eat therefore, and drink, and be comforted; and if thou shouldst see any mortal, say, "I have vowed to the All-merciful a fast, and today I will not speak to any man
t.hilali-khan: So eat and drink and be glad, and if you see any human being, say: 'Verily! I have vowed a fast unto the Most Beneficent (Allah) so I shall not speak to any human being this day. 
t.itani: “So eat, and drink, and be consoled. And if you see any human, say, ‘I have vowed a fast to the Most Gracious, so I will not speak to any human today.'“
t.mubarakpuri: "So eat and drink and rejoice. And if you see any human being, say: `Verily, I have vowed a fast for the Most Gracious so I shall not speak to any human being today.,"
t.qarib: So eat and drink and be satisfied and say to anyone you may see, I have made a vow of silence to the Lord of Mercy and I will not talk to anyone today
t.wahiduddin: Eat and drink and rejoice. And if you see any human being say, 'I have vowed a fast [of silence] to the Gracious God, and will not speak with any human being today
t.shakir: So eat and drink and refresh the eye. Then if you see any mortal, say: Surely I have vowed a fast to the Beneficent Allah, so I shall not speak to any man today
t.haleem: so eat, drink, be glad, and say to anyone you may see: “I have vowed to the Lord of Mercy to abstain from conversation, and I will not talk to anyone today.”’
t.maariful: So eat, drink and cool your eyes. Then if you see any human being, say (to him), “I have vowed a fast (of silence) for the All-Merciful (Allah,) and therefore, I shall never speak to any human today.”
t.khattab: So eat and drink, and put your heart at ease. But if you see any of the people, say, ‘I have vowed silence to the Most Compassionate, so I am not talking to anyone today.’”
t.ghali: So eat and drink and comfort your eye, yet in case you ever definitely see any mortal, (Literally: any of the mortals) then say, "Surely I have vowed fasting to The All-Merciful, so I will never speak today to any human being
t.irving: Eat and drink, and refresh yourself. Should you see even a single human being, then say: "I have vowed to keep a fast to the Mercy-giving whereby I´ll never speak to any person today!"´
t.iwq: So eat and drink and be contented. And if you see from among humanity anyone, say, 'Indeed, I have vowed to the Ever-Merciful abstention, so I will not speak today to any man

t_id.kemenag: maka makan, minum dan bersenang hatilah kamu. Jika kamu melihat seorang manusia, maka katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya aku telah bernazar berpuasa untuk Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka aku tidak akan berbicara dengan seorang manusiapun pada hari ini".
t_id.sabiq: Makanlah dari buah kurma itu dan minumlah serta bersenang-senanglah. Jika kamu melihat seseorang mengingkari perkara ini, maka berilah isyarat bahwa kamu sedang berpuasa bicara dan tidak akan berbicara kepada siapa pun hari itu.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka makanlah) dari buah kurma yang masak itu (dan minumlah) dari air sungai kecil itu (serta bersenang hatilah kamu) dengan anakmu itu. Lafal 'Ainan berfungsi sebagai Tamyiz yang dipindahkan dari Fa'ilnya, maksudnya selamat bersenang hati dengan anakmu itu. Atau dengan kata lain, kamu menjadi tenang dengan adanya anakmu itu sehingga kamu tidak memikirkan hal-hal yang lain. (Jika) lafal Immaa ini pada asalnya terdiri dari In Syarthiyah dan Ma Zaidah yang kemudian diidgamkan menjadi satu hingga menjadi Immaa (kamu melihat) dan lafal Tarayinna terbuang huruf Lam Fi'il dan 'Ain Fi'ilnya, kemudian harakat 'Ain Fi'ilnya dipindahkan pada huruf Ra, selanjutnya Ya Dhamir dikasrahkan karena bertemu dua sukun, sehingga jadilah Tarayinna (seorang manusia) kemudian ia menanyakan kepadamu tentang anakmu itu (maka katakanlah, "Sesungguhnya aku bernazar shaum untuk Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) dengan menahan diri untuk tidak berbicara, baik mengenai perihal anaknya atau orang-orang lainnya, hal ini terbukti dengan perkataan selanjutnya (maka aku tidak akan berkata dengan seorang manusia pun pada hari ini)" sesudah kejadian ini.
t_id.iwq: maka makan, minum dan bersenang hatilah kamu. Jika kamu melihat seorang manusia, maka katakanlah: "Sesungguhnya aku telah bernazar berpuasa untuk Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka aku tidak akan berbicara dengan seorang manusiapun pada hari ini".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فكلي» من الرطب «واشربي» من السري «وقري عينا» بالولد تمييز محول من الفاعل أي: لتقر عينك به أي: تسكن فلا تطمح إلى غيره «فإما» فيه إدغام نون إن الشرطية في ما الزائدة «ترين» حذفت منه لام الفعل وعينه وألقيت حركتها على الراء وكسرت ياء الضمير لالتقاء الساكنين «من البشر أحدا» فيسألك عن ولدك «فقولي إني نذرت للرحمن صوما» أي إمساكا عن الكلام في شأنه وغيره من الأناسي بدليل «فلن أكلم اليوم إنسا» أي: بعد ذلك
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَكُلِي وَٱشۡرَبِي وَقَرِّي عَيۡنٗاۖ فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ ٱلۡبَشَرِ أَحَدٗا فَقُولِيٓ إِنِّي نَذَرۡتُ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ صَوۡمٗا فَلَنۡ أُكَلِّمَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ إِنسِيّٗا (وَقَرِّي عَيْنًا: وَطِيبِي نَفْسًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka makanlah) dari buah kurma yang masak itu (dan minumlah) dari air sungai kecil itu (serta bersenang hatilah kamu) dengan anakmu itu. Lafal 'Ainan berfungsi sebagai Tamyiz yang dipindahkan dari Fa'ilnya, maksudnya selamat bersenang hati dengan anakmu itu. Atau dengan kata lain, kamu menjadi tenang dengan adanya anakmu itu sehingga kamu tidak memikirkan hal-hal yang lain. (Jika) lafal Immaa ini pada asalnya terdiri dari In Syarthiyah dan Ma Zaidah yang kemudian diidgamkan menjadi satu hingga menjadi Immaa (kamu melihat) dan lafal Tarayinna terbuang huruf Lam Fi'il dan 'Ain Fi'ilnya, kemudian harakat 'Ain Fi'ilnya dipindahkan pada huruf Ra, selanjutnya Ya Dhamir dikasrahkan karena bertemu dua sukun, sehingga jadilah Tarayinna (seorang manusia) kemudian ia menanyakan kepadamu tentang anakmu itu (maka katakanlah, "Sesungguhnya aku bernazar shaum untuk Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) dengan menahan diri untuk tidak berbicara, baik mengenai perihal anaknya atau orang-orang lainnya, hal ini terbukti dengan perkataan selanjutnya (maka aku tidak akan berkata dengan seorang manusia pun pada hari ini)" sesudah kejadian ini

ar: فَأَتَتۡ بِهِۦ قَوۡمَهَا تَحۡمِلُهُۥۖ قَالُوا۟ یَـٰمَرۡیَمُ لَقَدۡ جِئۡتِ شَیۡءࣰا فَرِیࣰّا
ar_simple: فَأَتَتْ بِهِ قَوْمَهَا تَحْمِلُهُ ۖ قَالُوا يَا مَرْيَمُ لَقَدْ جِئْتِ شَيْئًا فَرِيًّا
transliteration: fa-atat bihi qawmahā taḥmiluhu qālū yāmaryamu laqad ji'ti shayan fariyyan

t.sahih-int: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, "O Mary, you have certainly done a thing unprecedented
t.asad: And in time she returned to her people, carrying the child with her. They said: "O Mary! Thou hast indeed done an amazing thing
t.yusuf-ali: At length she brought the (babe) to her people, carrying him (in her arms). They said: "O Mary! truly an amazing thing hast thou brought
t.pickthall: Then she brought him to her own folk, carrying him. They said: O Mary! Thou hast come with an amazing thing
t.arberry: Then she brought the child to her folk carrying him; and they said, 'Mary, thou hast surely committed a monstrous thing
t.hilali-khan: Then she brought him (the baby) to her people, carrying him. They said: "O Mary! Indeed you have brought a thing Fariya (an unheard mighty thing)
t.itani: Then she came to her people, carrying him. They said, “O Mary, you have done something terrible.
t.mubarakpuri: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said: "O Mary! Indeed you have brought a thing Fariy."
t.qarib: Then she went back to her people, carrying him. They said, "Mary, you have done an unthinkable thing
t.wahiduddin: Carrying her child, she brought him to her people. They said, "O Mary, you have indeed done something terrible
t.shakir: And she came to her people with him, carrying him (with her). They said: O Marium! surely you have done a strange thing
t.haleem: She went back to her people carrying the child, and they said, ‘Mary! You have done something terrible
t.maariful: Then she came to her people carrying him (the baby). They said, “O Maryam you have committed something grave indeed
t.khattab: Then she returned to her people, carrying him. They said ˹in shock˺, “O Mary! You have certainly done a horrible thing
t.ghali: Then she came up with him to her people carrying him. They said, "O Maryam, (Mary) indeed you have readily come with a fabricated thing
t.irving: She carried him back to her family. They said: "Mary, you have brought something hard to believe
t.iwq: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said, "O Mary, you have certainly done a thing unprecedented

t_id.kemenag: Maka Maryam membawa anak itu kepada kaumnya dengan menggendongnya. Kaumnya berkata: "Hai Maryam, sesungguhnya kamu telah melakukan sesuatu yang amat mungkar.
t_id.sabiq: Maryam mendatangi kerabatnya sambil membawa 'Isâ. Dengan rasa heran dan ingkar, mereka berkata kepadanya, "Sesungguhnya kamu telah membawa sesuatu yang amat buruk dan mungkar."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Maryam membawa anak itu kepada kaumnya dengan menggendongnya) lafal Tahmiluhu menjadi Hal atau kata keterangan keadaan. Sehingga kaumnya melihat anak itu (Kaumnya berkata, "Hai Maryam! Sesungguhnya kamu telah melakukan sesuatu yang amat mungkar) suatu dosa yang sangat besar karena kamu memperoleh anak tanpa ayah.
t_id.iwq: Maka Maryam membawa anak itu kepada kaumnya dengan menggendongnya. Kaumnya berkata: "Hai Maryam, sesungguhnya kamu telah melakukan sesuatu yang amat mungkar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأتت به قومها تحمله» حال فرأوه «قالوا يا مريم لقد جئت شيئا فريا» عظيما حيث أتيت بولد من غير أب
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَتَتۡ بِهِۦ قَوۡمَهَا تَحۡمِلُهُۥۖ قَالُواْ يَٰمَرۡيَمُ لَقَدۡ جِئۡتِ شَيۡـٔٗا فَرِيّٗا (فَرِيًّا: أَمْرًا عَظِيمًا مُفْتَرًى)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Maryam membawa anak itu kepada kaumnya dengan menggendongnya) lafal Tahmiluhu menjadi Hal atau kata keterangan keadaan. Sehingga kaumnya melihat anak itu (Kaumnya berkata, "Hai Maryam! Sesungguhnya kamu telah melakukan sesuatu yang amat mungkar) suatu dosa yang sangat besar karena kamu memperoleh anak tanpa ayah

ar: یَـٰۤأُخۡتَ هَـٰرُونَ مَا كَانَ أَبُوكِ ٱمۡرَأَ سَوۡءࣲ وَمَا كَانَتۡ أُمُّكِ بَغِیࣰّا
ar_simple: يَا أُخْتَ هَارُونَ مَا كَانَ أَبُوكِ امْرَأَ سَوْءٍ وَمَا كَانَتْ أُمُّكِ بَغِيًّا
transliteration: yāukh'ta hārūna mā kāna abūki im'ra-a sawin wamā kānat ummuki baghiyyan

t.sahih-int: O sister of Aaron, your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste
t.asad: O sister of Aaron! Thy father was not a wicked man, nor was thy mother a loose woman
t.yusuf-ali: O sister of Aaron! Thy father was not a man of evil, nor thy mother a woman unchaste
t.pickthall: O sister of Aaron! Thy father was not a wicked man nor was thy mother a harlot
t.arberry: Sister of Aaron, thy father was not a wicked man, nor was thy mother a woman unchaste
t.hilali-khan: O sister (i.e. the like) of Harun (Aaron) [not the brother of Musa (Moses), but he was another pious man at the time of Maryam (Mary)]! Your father was not a man who used to commit adultery, nor your mother was an unchaste woman
t.itani: O sister of Aaron, your father was not an evil man, and your mother was not a whore.”
t.mubarakpuri: "O sister of Harun! Your father was not a man who used to commit adultery, nor your mother was an unchaste woman."
t.qarib: Sister of Aaron, your father was not a bad man, nor was your mother a loose woman
t.wahiduddin: Sister of Aaron, your father was not an evil man, nor was your mother an unchaste woman
t.shakir: O sister of Haroun! your father was not a bad man, nor, was your mother an unchaste woman
t.haleem: Sister of Aaron! Your father was not an evil man; your mother was not unchaste!’
t.maariful: O sister of Hārūn, neither your father was a man of evil nor was your mother unchaste.”
t.khattab: O sister of Aaron! Your father was not an indecent man, nor was your mother unchaste.”
t.ghali: O sister of Harun, (Aaron) in no way was your father a woeful person, and in no way was your mother a prostitute
t.irving: Kinswoman of Aaron, your father was no evil man, nor was your mother a loose woman
t.iwq: O sister of Aaron, your father was not a man of evil, nor was your mother unchaste

t_id.kemenag: Hai saudara perempuan Harun, ayahmu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang yang jahat dan ibumu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang pezina",
t_id.sabiq: "Wahai kerabat Hârûn, Nabi yang bertakwa dan taat," kata mereka lagi, "bagaimana kamu melakukan hal itu, padahal bapakmu sama sekali bukan orang yang tak bermoral, dan ibumu pun bukan seorang pezina."(1). (1) Dalam Ensiklopedia Britanica disebutkan bahwa al-Qur'ân telah melakukan kesalahan sejarah ketika mengatakan bahwa Maryam adalah saudara Hârûn seperti termaktub pada ayat di atas. Padahal antara Hârûn, saudara Mûsâ, dan Maryam terdapat jarak ratusan tahun. Ensiklopedi itu lupa bahwa arti "saudara" di sini bukan dalam arti hubungan darah, tetapi "saudara" atas dasar kesamaan sikap, seperti disebut dalam kamus Lisân al-'Arab. Dengan demikian, sebutan bahwa Maryam saudara Hârûn adalah saudara dalam arti orang yang mempunyai kesamaan sifat kebaikan dan ketakwaan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Hai saudara perempuan Harun!) dia adalah seorang lelaki yang saleh, hal ini berarti Maryam pun serupa dengannya dalam hal memelihara kehormatan (Ayahmu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang yang buruk) bukan seorang pezina (dan ibumu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang pelacur)" bukan pula seorang pezina, maka dari manakah anak ini.
t_id.iwq: Hai saudara perempuan Harun, ayahmu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang yang jahat dan ibumu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang pezina",

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا أخت هارون» هو رجل صالح أي: يا شبيهته في العفة «ما كان أبوك أمرأ سوء» أي: زانيا «وما كانت أمك بغيا» زانية فمن أين لك هذا الولد
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَـٰٓأُخۡتَ هَٰرُونَ مَا كَانَ أَبُوكِ ٱمۡرَأَ سَوۡءٖ وَمَا كَانَتۡ أُمُّكِ بَغِيّٗا (بَغِيًّا: زَانِيَةً)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Hai saudara perempuan Harun!) dia adalah seorang lelaki yang saleh, hal ini berarti Maryam pun serupa dengannya dalam hal memelihara kehormatan (Ayahmu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang yang buruk) bukan seorang pezina (dan ibumu sekali-kali bukanlah seorang pelacur)" bukan pula seorang pezina, maka dari manakah anak ini

ar: فَأَشَارَتۡ إِلَیۡهِۖ قَالُوا۟ كَیۡفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَن كَانَ فِی ٱلۡمَهۡدِ صَبِیࣰّا
ar_simple: فَأَشَارَتْ إِلَيْهِ ۖ قَالُوا كَيْفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَنْ كَانَ فِي الْمَهْدِ صَبِيًّا
transliteration: fa-ashārat ilayhi qālū kayfa nukallimu man kāna fī l-mahdi ṣabiyyan

t.sahih-int: So she pointed to him. They said, "How can we speak to one who is in the cradle a child
t.asad: Thereupon she pointed to him. They exclaimed: "How can we talk to one who [as yet] is a little boy in the cradle
t.yusuf-ali: But she pointed to the babe. They said: "How can we talk to one who is a child in the cradle
t.pickthall: Then she pointed to him. They said: How can we talk to one who is in the cradle, a young boy
t.arberry: Mary pointed to the child then; but they said, 'How shall we speak to one who is still in the cradle, a little child
t.hilali-khan: Then she pointed to him. They said: "How can we talk to one who is a child in the cradle
t.itani: So she pointed to him. They said, “How can we speak to an infant in the crib?”
t.mubarakpuri: Then she pointed to him. They said: "How can we talk to one who is a child in the cradle"
t.qarib: She pointed to him. They said, "How can we speak to a child in the cradle
t.wahiduddin: She pointed to the child. They said, "How shall we talk to someone who is a child in the cradle
t.shakir: But she pointed to him. They said: How should we speak to one who was a child in the cradle
t.haleem: She pointed at him. They said, ‘How can we converse with an infant?’
t.maariful: So, she pointed towards him (the baby). They said, “How shall we speak to someone who is still a child in the cradle?”
t.khattab: So she pointed to the baby. They exclaimed, “How can we talk to someone who is an infant in the cradle?”
t.ghali: So she pointed to him. They said, "How shall we speak to one who is (still) in the cradle, a young boy
t.irving: She pointed to him. They said: "How shall we talk to someone who is a child in the cradle
t.iwq: Thus she pointed to him. They said, "How can we speak to one who is in the cradle a child

t_id.kemenag: maka Maryam menunjuk kepada anaknya. Mereka berkata: "Bagaimana kami akan berbicara dengan anak kecil yang masih di dalam ayunan?"
t_id.sabiq: Maryam menunjuk anaknya, 'Isâ a. s., supaya mereka berbicara langsung kepadanya. Lalu mereka berkata, "Bagaimana kami dapat berbicara dengan anak kecil yang masih dalam buaian?"
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka Maryam mengisyaratkan) kepada kaumnya (-seraya menunjuk- kepada anaknya) maksudnya supaya mereka bertanya kepada anaknya. (Mereka berkata, "Bagaimana kami akan berbicara dengan anak) yang masih (kecil berada dalam ayunan?)".
t_id.iwq: maka Maryam menunjuk kepada anaknya. Mereka berkata: "Bagaimana kami akan berbicara dengan anak kecil yang masih di dalam ayunan?"

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فأشارت» لهم «إليه» أن كلموه «قالوا كيف نكلم من كان» أي وجد «في المهد صبيا
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَأَشَارَتۡ إِلَيۡهِۖ قَالُواْ كَيۡفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَن كَانَ فِي ٱلۡمَهۡدِ صَبِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka Maryam mengisyaratkan) kepada kaumnya (-seraya menunjuk- kepada anaknya) maksudnya supaya mereka bertanya kepada anaknya. (Mereka berkata, "Bagaimana kami akan berbicara dengan anak) yang masih (kecil berada dalam ayunan)

ar: قَالَ إِنِّی عَبۡدُ ٱللَّهِ ءَاتَىٰنِیَ ٱلۡكِتَـٰبَ وَجَعَلَنِی نَبِیࣰّا
ar_simple: قَالَ إِنِّي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ آتَانِيَ الْكِتَابَ وَجَعَلَنِي نَبِيًّا
transliteration: qāla innī ʿabdu l-lahi ātāniya l-kitāba wajaʿalanī nabiyyan

t.sahih-int: [Jesus] said, "Indeed, I am the servant of Allah. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet
t.asad: [But] he said: "Behold, I am a servant of God. He has vouchsafed unto me revelation and made me a prophet
t.yusuf-ali: He said: "I am indeed a servant of Allah: He hath given me revelation and made me a prophet
t.pickthall: He spake: Lo! I am the slave of Allah. He hath given me the Scripture and hath appointed me a Prophet
t.arberry: He said, 'Lo, I am God's servant; God has given me the Book, and made me a Prophet
t.hilali-khan: He ['Iesa (Jesus)] said: Verily! I am a slave of Allah, He has given me the Scripture and made me a Prophet
t.itani: He said, “I am the servant of God. He has given me the Scripture, and made me a prophet.
t.mubarakpuri: He said: "Verily, I am a servant of Allah, He has given me the Scripture and made me a Prophet;"
t.qarib: [Jesus] said, "I am the servant of God. He has given me the Book and made me a prophet
t.wahiduddin: [But] he said, "I am God's servant. He has given me the Book and made me a prophet
t.shakir: He said: Surely I am a servant of Allah; He has given me the Book and made me a prophet
t.haleem: [But] he said: ‘I am a servant of God. He has granted me the Scripture; made me a prophet
t.maariful: Spoke he (the baby), “Verily I am the servant of Allah. He has given me the Book, and made me a prophet
t.khattab: ˹Jesus˺ declared, “I am truly a servant of Allah. He has destined me to be given the Scripture and to be a prophet
t.ghali: He said, "Surely I am the bondman of Allah. He has brought me the Book and made me a Prophet
t.irving: He said: "I am God´s servant. He has given me the Book and made me a prophet
t.iwq: Jesus said, "Indeed, I am the servant of Allah. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Isa: "Sesungguhnya aku ini hamba Allah, Dia memberiku Al Kitab (Injil) dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang nabi,
t_id.sabiq: Ketika 'Isâ mendengar perkataaan mereka, Allah lalu menjadikannya dapat berbicara, "Sesungguhnya aku adalah hamba Allah yang akan diberi kitab Injîl. Allah akan memilihku menjadi seorang nabi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Isa berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku ini hamba Allah, Dia memberiku Alkitab) yakni kitab Injil (dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang nabi).
t_id.iwq: Berkata Isa: "Sesungguhnya aku ini hamba Allah, Dia memberiku Al Kitab (Injil) dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang nabi,

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال إني عبد الله آتاني الكتاب» أي: الإنجيل «وجعلني نبيا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ إِنِّي عَبۡدُ ٱللَّهِ ءَاتَىٰنِيَ ٱلۡكِتَٰبَ وَجَعَلَنِي نَبِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Isa berkata, "Sesungguhnya aku ini hamba Allah, Dia memberiku Alkitab) yakni kitab Injil (dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang nabi)

ar: وَجَعَلَنِی مُبَارَكًا أَیۡنَ مَا كُنتُ وَأَوۡصَـٰنِی بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ مَا دُمۡتُ حَیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَجَعَلَنِي مُبَارَكًا أَيْنَ مَا كُنْتُ وَأَوْصَانِي بِالصَّلَاةِ وَالزَّكَاةِ مَا دُمْتُ حَيًّا
transliteration: wajaʿalanī mubārakan ayna mā kuntu wa-awṣānī bil-ṣalati wal-zakati mā dum'tu ḥayyan

t.sahih-int: And He has made me blessed wherever I am and has enjoined upon me prayer and zakah as long as I remain alive
t.asad: and made me blessed wherever I may be; and He has enjoined upon me prayer and charity as long as I live
t.yusuf-ali: And He hath made me blessed wheresoever I be, and hath enjoined on me Prayer and Charity as long as I live
t.pickthall: And hath made me blessed wheresoever I may be, and hath enjoined upon me prayer and almsgiving so long as I remain alive
t.arberry: Blessed He has made me, wherever I may be; and He has enjoined me to pray, and to give the alms, so long as I live
t.hilali-khan: And He has made me blessed wheresoever I be, and has enjoined on me Salat (prayer), and Zakat, as long as I live
t.itani: And has made me blessed wherever I may be; and has enjoined on me prayer and charity, so long as I live.
t.mubarakpuri: "And He has made me blessed wherever I be, and has enjoined on me [Awsani] Salah and Zakah, as long as I live."
t.qarib: And He has made me blessed wherever I am and directed me to pray and to give charitable gifts as long as I live
t.wahiduddin: He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and has enjoined upon me prayer and almsgiving throughout my life
t.shakir: And He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and He has enjoined on me prayer and poor-rate so long as I live
t.haleem: made me blessed wherever I may be. He commanded me to pray, to give alms as long as I live
t.maariful: and He has made me a blessed one wherever I be, and has enjoined Salāh and Zakāh upon me as long as I am alive
t.khattab: He has made me a blessing wherever I go, and bid me to establish prayer and give alms-tax as long as I live
t.ghali: And He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and He has enjoined upon me prayer and Zakat (The poordues) as long as I am alive
t.irving: He has made me blessed wherever I may be, and commissioned me to pray and [pay] the welfare tax so long as I live
t.iwq: And He has made me blessed wherever I am and has enjoined upon me prayer and zakah as long as I remain alive

t_id.kemenag: dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang yang diberkati di mana saja aku berada, dan Dia memerintahkan kepadaku (mendirikan) shalat dan (menunaikan) zakat selama aku hidup;
t_id.sabiq: Allah menjadikan aku sebagai orang yang diberkati, mengajarkan kebaikan dan mendatangkan kebaikan bagi manusia. Dia memerintahkanku untuk melakukan salat dan menunaikan zakat selama hidupku.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang yang diberkati di mana saja aku berada) maksudnya Dia menjadikan diriku orang yang banyak memberi manfaat kepada manusia. Ungkapan ini merupakan berita tentang kedudukan yang telah dipastikan baginya (dan Dia memerintahkan kepadaku mendirikan salat dan menunaikan zakat) Allah memerintahkan kepadaku untuk melakukan kedua hal tersebut (selama aku hidup).
t_id.iwq: dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang yang diberkati di mana saja aku berada, dan Dia memerintahkan kepadaku (mendirikan) salat dan (menunaikan) zakat selama aku hidup;

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وجعلني مباركا أينما كنت» أي: نفاعا للناس إخبار بما كتب له «وأوصاني بالصلاة والزكاة» أمرني بهما «ما دمت حيا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَجَعَلَنِي مُبَارَكًا أَيۡنَ مَا كُنتُ وَأَوۡصَٰنِي بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ مَا دُمۡتُ حَيّٗا (مُبَارَكًا: عَظِيمَ الخَيْرِ وَالنَّفْعِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Dia menjadikan aku seorang yang diberkati di mana saja aku berada) maksudnya Dia menjadikan diriku orang yang banyak memberi manfaat kepada manusia. Ungkapan ini merupakan berita tentang kedudukan yang telah dipastikan baginya (dan Dia memerintahkan kepadaku mendirikan salat dan menunaikan zakat) Allah memerintahkan kepadaku untuk melakukan kedua hal tersebut (selama aku hidup)

ar: وَبَرَّۢا بِوَ ٰلِدَتِی وَلَمۡ یَجۡعَلۡنِی جَبَّارࣰا شَقِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَبَرًّا بِوَالِدَتِي وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْنِي جَبَّارًا شَقِيًّا
transliteration: wabarran biwālidatī walam yajʿalnī jabbāran shaqiyyan

t.sahih-int: And [made me] dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a wretched tyrant
t.asad: and [has endowed me with] piety towards my mother; and He has not made me haughty or bereft of grace
t.yusuf-ali: (He) hath made me kind to my mother, and not overbearing or miserable
t.pickthall: And (hath made me) dutiful toward her who bore me, and hath not made me arrogant, unblest
t.arberry: and likewise to cherish my mother; He has not made me arrogant, unprosperous
t.hilali-khan: And dutiful to my mother, and made me not arrogant, unblest
t.itani: And kind to my mother, and He did not make me a disobedient rebel.
t.mubarakpuri: "And to be dutiful to my mother, and made me not arrogant, unblessed."
t.qarib: and to be full of kindness toward .mother, and He has not made me domineering or rebellious
t.wahiduddin: He has made me dutiful toward my mother, and He has not made me arrogant or wicked
t.shakir: And dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me insolent, unblessed
t.haleem: to cherish my mother. He did not make me domineering or graceless
t.maariful: and (He has made me) good to my mother, and he did not make me oppressive (or) ill-fated
t.khattab: and to be kind to my mother. He has not made me arrogant or defiant
t.ghali: And ever benign to my (female) parent; (Literally: She who gave birt to me) and He has not made me arrogant, wretched
t.irving: and [to act] considerate towards my mother. He has not made me domineering, hard to get along with
t.iwq: And made me dutiful to my mother, and He has not made me a wretched tyrant

t_id.kemenag: dan berbakti kepada ibuku, dan Dia tidak menjadikan aku seorang yang sombong lagi celaka.
t_id.sabiq: Dia juga memerintahkanku untuk berbakti kepada ibuku, dan tidak menjadikan aku bersikap aniaya kepada manusia, serta celaka karena berbuat maksiat kepada-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan berbakti kepada ibuku) lafal Barran dinashabkan oleh lafal Ja'alani yang keberadaannya diperkirakan, maksudnya, Dia menjadikan aku sebagai orang yang berbakti kepada ibuku (dan Dia tidak menjadikan aku seorang yang sombong) orang yang merasa tinggi diri (lagi celaka) yang durhaka kepada Rabbnya.
t_id.iwq: dan berbakti kepada ibuku, dan Dia tidak menjadikan aku seorang yang sombong lagi celaka.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وبرا بوالدتي» منصوب بجعلني مقدرا «ولم يجعلني جبارا» متعاظما «شقيا» عاصيا لربه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَبَرَّۢا بِوَٰلِدَتِي وَلَمۡ يَجۡعَلۡنِي جَبَّارٗا شَقِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan berbakti kepada ibuku) lafal Barran dinashabkan oleh lafal Ja'alani yang keberadaannya diperkirakan, maksudnya, Dia menjadikan aku sebagai orang yang berbakti kepada ibuku (dan Dia tidak menjadikan aku seorang yang sombong) orang yang merasa tinggi diri (lagi celaka) yang durhaka kepada Rabbnya

ar: وَٱلسَّلَـٰمُ عَلَیَّ یَوۡمَ وُلِدتُّ وَیَوۡمَ أَمُوتُ وَیَوۡمَ أُبۡعَثُ حَیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَالسَّلَامُ عَلَيَّ يَوْمَ وُلِدْتُ وَيَوْمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ أُبْعَثُ حَيًّا
transliteration: wal-salāmu ʿalayya yawma wulidttu wayawma amūtu wayawma ub'ʿathu ḥayyan

t.sahih-int: And peace is on me the day I was born and the day I will die and the day I am raised alive
t.asad: Hence, peace was upon me on the day when I was born, and [will be upon me] on the day of my death, and on the day when I shall be raised to life [again]
t.yusuf-ali: So peace is on me the day I was born, the day that I die, and the day that I shall be raised up to life (again)
t.pickthall: Peace on me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I shall be raised alive
t.arberry: Peace be upon me, the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am raised up alive
t.hilali-khan: And Salam (peace) be upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I shall be raised alive
t.itani: So Peace is upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the Day I get resurrected alive.”
t.mubarakpuri: "And Salam (peace) be upon me the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I shall be raised alive!"
t.qarib: And [God's] peace was upon me the day I was born and the day I will die and it will be upon me the day I am raised alive
t.wahiduddin: Blessed was I on the day I was born, and blessed I shall be on the day I die and on the day I am raised to life again
t.shakir: And peace on me on the day I was born, and on the day I die, and on the day I am raised to life
t.haleem: Peace was on me the day I was born, and will be on me the day I die and the day I am raised to life again.’
t.maariful: And peace is upon me the day I was born, the day I shall die, and the day I shall be raised alive again.”
t.khattab: Peace be upon me the day I was born, the day I die, and the day I will be raised back to life!”
t.ghali: And peace be upon me, the day I was born, and the day I die, and the day I am made to rise again alive
t.irving: Peace be on the day I was born, and the day I shall die and the day I am raised to life again
t.iwq: And peace is on me the day I was born and the day I will die and the day I am raised alive

t_id.kemenag: Dan kesejahteraan semoga dilimpahkan kepadaku, pada hari aku dilahirkan, pada hari aku meninggal dan pada hari aku dibangkitkan hidup kembali".
t_id.sabiq: Aku akan memperoleh keamanan dari Allah pada hari kelahiranku, hari kematianku dan hari kebangkitanku kembali dari kematian."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan kesejahteraan) dari Allah (semoga dilimpahkan kepadaku, pada hari aku dilahirkan, pada hari aku meninggal dan pada hari aku dibangkitkan hidup kembali)" hal ini telah dikatakan pada kisah yang lalu, yaitu dalam doa Nabi Yahya.
t_id.iwq: Dan kesejahteraan semoga dilimpahkan kepadaku, pada hari aku dilahirkan, pada hari aku meninggal dan pada hari aku dibangkitkan hidup kembali".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «والسلام» من الله «عليّ يوم ولدت ويوم أموت ويوم أبعث حيا» يقال فيه ما تقدم في السيد يحيى. قال تعالى
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱلسَّلَٰمُ عَلَيَّ يَوۡمَ وُلِدتُّ وَيَوۡمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوۡمَ أُبۡعَثُ حَيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan kesejahteraan) dari Allah (semoga dilimpahkan kepadaku, pada hari aku dilahirkan, pada hari aku meninggal dan pada hari aku dibangkitkan hidup kembali)" hal ini telah dikatakan pada kisah yang lalu, yaitu dalam doa Nabi Yahya

ar: ذَ ٰلِكَ عِیسَى ٱبۡنُ مَرۡیَمَۖ قَوۡلَ ٱلۡحَقِّ ٱلَّذِی فِیهِ یَمۡتَرُونَ
ar_simple: ذَٰلِكَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ ۚ قَوْلَ الْحَقِّ الَّذِي فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ
transliteration: dhālika ʿīsā ub'nu maryama qawla l-ḥaqi alladhī fīhi yamtarūna

t.sahih-int: That is Jesus, the son of Mary - the word of truth about which they are in dispute
t.asad: SUCH WAS, in the words of truth, Jesus the son of Mary, about whose nature they so deeply disagree
t.yusuf-ali: Such (was) Jesus the son of Mary: (it is) a statement of truth, about which they (vainly) dispute
t.pickthall: Such was Jesus, son of Mary: (this is) a statement of the truth concerning which they doubt
t.arberry: That is Jesus, son of Mary, in word of truth, concerning which they are doubting
t.hilali-khan: Such is 'Iesa (Jesus), son of Maryam (Mary). (it is) a statement of truth, about which they doubt (or dispute)
t.itani: That is Jesus son of Mary—the Word of truth about which they doubt.
t.mubarakpuri: Such is `Isa, son of Maryam. Qawlal-Haqq [it is a statement of truth] about which they doubt (or dispute).
t.qarib: Such is Jesus, the son of Mary - the Word of Truth about which they are in dispute
t.wahiduddin: Such was Jesus, the son of Mary. That is the whole truth, about which they still dispute
t.shakir: Such is Isa, son of Marium; (this is) the saying of truth about which they dispute
t.haleem: Such was Jesus, son of Mary. [This is] a statement of the Truth about which they are in doubt
t.maariful: That is ‘Īsā, the son of Maryam, to say the truth in which they (Christians) are disputing
t.khattab: That is Jesus, son of Mary. ˹And this is˺ a word of truth, about which they dispute
t.ghali: That is Isa (Jesus) son of Maryam, in word of truth, concerning which they are wrangling
t.irving: Such was Jesus, the son of Mary; [it is] a true statement which they are still puzzling over
t.iwq: That is Jesus, the son of Mary - the word of truth about which they are in dispute

t_id.kemenag: Itulah Isa putera Maryam, yang mengatakan perkataan yang benar, yang mereka berbantah-bantahan tentang kebenarannya.
t_id.sabiq: Orang yang bersifat seperti itu adalah 'Isâ putra Maryam. Inilah pernyataan yang benar tentang dirinya yang banyak dipertentangkan oleh orang-orang yang mendustakannya dan yang kenabiannya diragukan oleh banyak orang.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Itulah Isa putra Maryam, yang mengatakan perkataan yang benar) jika lafal Alqaul dibaca Rafa', berarti menjadi Khabar dari Mubtada yang diperkirakan keberadaannya, maksudnya, perkataan Isa bin Maryam adalah perkataan yang benar. Kalau dibaca Nashab berarti ada lafal Qultu yang diperkirakan keberadaannya sebelumnya, maksudnya Aku mengatakan perkataan yang benar (yang mereka berbantah-bantahan tentang kebenarannya) lafal Yamtaruuna berasal dari kata Al Miryah; mereka meragukan kebenarannya, mereka adalah orang-orang Nasrani; mereka mengatakan perkataan yang dusta, yaitu, "Sesungguhnya Isa itu adalah anak Allah".
t_id.iwq: Itulah Isa putera Maryam, yang mengatakan perkataan yang benar, yang mereka berbantah-bantahan tentang kebenarannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ذلك عيسى ابن مريم قولُ الحق» بالرفع خبر مبتدأ مقدر أي: قول ابن مريم وبالنصب بتقدير قلت، والمعنى القول الحق «الذي فيه يمترون» من المرية أي: يشكون وهم النصارى: قالوا إن عيسى ابن الله، كذبوا
tafsir.siraj-ar: ذَٰلِكَ عِيسَى ٱبۡنُ مَرۡيَمَۖ قَوۡلَ ٱلۡحَقِّ ٱلَّذِي فِيهِ يَمۡتَرُونَ (يَمْتَرُونَ: يَشُكُّونَ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Itulah Isa putra Maryam, yang mengatakan perkataan yang benar) jika lafal Alqaul dibaca Rafa', berarti menjadi Khabar dari Mubtada yang diperkirakan keberadaannya, maksudnya, perkataan Isa bin Maryam adalah perkataan yang benar. Kalau dibaca Nashab berarti ada lafal Qultu yang diperkirakan keberadaannya sebelumnya, maksudnya Aku mengatakan perkataan yang benar (yang mereka berbantah-bantahan tentang kebenarannya) lafal Yamtaruuna berasal dari kata Al Miryah; mereka meragukan kebenarannya, mereka adalah orang-orang Nasrani; mereka mengatakan perkataan yang dusta, yaitu, "Sesungguhnya Isa itu adalah anak Allah

ar: مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَن یَتَّخِذَ مِن وَلَدࣲۖ سُبۡحَـٰنَهُۥۤۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰۤ أَمۡرࣰا فَإِنَّمَا یَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَیَكُونُ
ar_simple: مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَنْ يَتَّخِذَ مِنْ وَلَدٍ ۖ سُبْحَانَهُ ۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ
transliteration: mā kāna lillahi an yattakhidha min waladin sub'ḥānahu idhā qaḍā amran fa-innamā yaqūlu lahu kun fayakūnu

t.sahih-int: It is not [befitting] for Allah to take a son; exalted is He! When He decrees an affair, He only says to it, "Be," and it is
t.asad: It is not conceivable that God should have taken unto Himself a son: limitless is He in His glory! When He wills a thing to be, He but says unto it "Be" -and it is
t.yusuf-ali: It is not befitting to (the majesty of) Allah that He should beget a son. Glory be to Him! when He determines a matter, He only says to it, "Be", and it is
t.pickthall: It befitteth not (the Majesty of) Allah that He should take unto Himself a son. Glory be to Him! When He decreeth a thing, He saith unto it only: Be! and it is
t.arberry: It is not for God to take a son unto Him. Glory be to Him! When He decrees a thing, He but says to it 'Be,' and it is
t.hilali-khan: It befits not (the Majesty of) Allah that He should beget a son [this refers to the slander of Christians against Allah, by saying that 'Iesa (Jesus) is the son of Allah]. Glorified (and Exalted be He above all that they associate with Him). When He decrees a thing, He only says to it, "Be!" and it is
t.itani: It is not for God to have a child—glory be to Him. To have anything done, He says to it, “Be,” and it becomes.
t.mubarakpuri: It befits not (the majesty of) Allah that He should beget a son. Glorified be He. When He decrees a thing, He only says to it: "Be!" -- and it is.
t.qarib: It does not befit God to have a son; may He be exalted in His glory! When He decides on something, He simply says to it, "Be!" and it is
t.wahiduddin: it does not befit the majesty of God that He should beget a son. Glory be to Him! He is far above that: when He decrees something, He says only, "Be!" and it is
t.shakir: It beseems not Allah that He should take to Himself a son, glory to be Him; when He has decreed a matter He only says to it "Be," and it is
t.haleem: it would not befit God to have a child. He is far above that: when He decrees something, He says only, ‘Be,’ and it is
t.maariful: It is not for Allah to have a son. Pure is He. When He decides a thing, He simply says to it, “Be” and it comes to be
t.khattab: It is not for Allah to take a son! Glory be to Him. When He decrees a matter, He simply tells it, “Be!” And it is
t.ghali: In no way is it for Allah to take to Him a child. All Extolment be to Him! When He decrees a Command, then He only says to it, "Be!" and it is
t.irving: It is not God´s role to adopt a son. Glory be to Him! Whenever He determines upon some matter, He merely tells it: "Be!", and it is
t.iwq: It is not befitting for Allah to take a son; exalted is He! When He decrees an affair, He only says to it, "Be," and it is

t_id.kemenag: Tidak layak bagi Allah mempunyai anak, Maha Suci Dia. Apabila Dia telah menetapkan sesuatu, maka Dia hanya berkata kepadanya: "Jadilah", maka jadilah ia.
t_id.sabiq: Tidak benar bahwa Allah mempunyai anak--Mahasuci Allah dari hal itu. Apabila Dia telah menetapkan sesuatu, niscaya kehendak-Nya itu akan terlaksana. Tidak ada yang mustahil bagi Allah. Dengan berkata, "Jadilah," maka akan terwujud makhluk yang dikehendaki-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Tidak layak bagi Allah mempunyai anak, Maha Suci Dia) dari hal tersebut. (Apabila Dia telah menetapkan sesuatu) yakni, Dia berkehendak untuk menciptakannya (maka Dia hanya berkata kepadanya, "Jadilah" maka jadilah dia) kalau dibaca Rafa' yaitu Yakuunu berarti ada lafal Huwa atau dia yang diperkirakan keberadaannya, kalau dibaca Nashab yaitu berarti dengan memperkirakan lafal An sebelumnya. Oleh sebab itu maka Nabi Isa diciptakan tanpa ayah.
t_id.iwq: Tidak layak bagi Allah mempunyai anak, Maha Suci Dia. Apabila Dia telah menetapkan sesuatu, maka Dia hanya berkata kepadanya: "Jadilah", maka jadilah ia.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ما كان لله أن يتخذ من ولد سبحانه» تنزيها له عن ذلك «إذا قضى أمرا» أي: أراد أن يحدثه «فإنما يقول له كن في فيكونُ» بالرفع بتقدير هو، وبالنصب بتقدير أن ومن ذلك خلق عيسى من غير أب
tafsir.siraj-ar: مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ مِن وَلَدٖۖ سُبۡحَٰنَهُۥٓۚ إِذَا قَضَىٰٓ أَمۡرٗا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُۥ كُن فَيَكُونُ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Tidak layak bagi Allah mempunyai anak, Maha Suci Dia) dari hal tersebut. (Apabila Dia telah menetapkan sesuatu) yakni, Dia berkehendak untuk menciptakannya (maka Dia hanya berkata kepadanya, "Jadilah" maka jadilah dia) kalau dibaca Rafa' yaitu Yakuunu berarti ada lafal Huwa atau dia yang diperkirakan keberadaannya, kalau dibaca Nashab yaitu berarti dengan memperkirakan lafal An sebelumnya. Oleh sebab itu maka Nabi Isa diciptakan tanpa ayah

ar: وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّی وَرَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُوهُۚ هَـٰذَا صِرَ ٰطࣱ مُّسۡتَقِیمࣱ
ar_simple: وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُوهُ ۚ هَٰذَا صِرَاطٌ مُسْتَقِيمٌ
transliteration: wa-inna l-laha rabbī warabbukum fa-uʿ'budūhu hādhā ṣirāṭun mus'taqīmun

t.sahih-int: [Jesus said], "And indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path
t.asad: And [thus it was that Jesus always said]: "Verily, God is my Sustainer as well as your Sustainer; so worship [none but] Him: this (alone] is a straight way
t.yusuf-ali: Verily Allah is my Lord and your Lord: Him therefore serve ye: this is a Way that is straight
t.pickthall: And lo! Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So serve Him. That is the right path
t.arberry: Surely God is my Lord, and your Lord; So serve you Him. This is a straight path
t.hilali-khan: Iesa (Jesus) said]: "And verily Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him (Alone). That is the Straight Path. (Allah's Religion of Islamic Monotheism which He did ordain for all of His Prophets)." [Tafsir At-Tabari]
t.itani: “God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path.”
t.mubarakpuri: [`Isa said]: "And verily, Allah is my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him. That is the straight path."
t.qarib: [Jesus continued]: "God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path
t.wahiduddin: God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him alone. That is the right path
t.shakir: And surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord, therefore serve Him; this is the right path
t.haleem: ‘God is my Lord and your Lord, so serve Him: that is a straight path.’
t.maariful: And (O Prophet, say to the people,) “Allah is surely my Lord and your Lord. So, worship Him. This is the straight path.”
t.khattab: ˹Jesus also declared,˺ “Surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him ˹alone˺. This is the Straight Path.”
t.ghali: And surely Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. This is a straight Path
t.irving: God is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him [Alone]. This is a Straight Road [to follow]
t.iwq: Jesus said, "And indeed, Allah is my Lord and your Lord, so worship Him. That is a straight path

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Allah adalah Tuhanku dan Tuhanmu, maka sembahIah Dia oleh kamu sekalian. Ini adalah jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Sesungguhnya Allah adalah Tuhanku dan Tuhan kalian. Maka sembahlah Dia, dan jangan sekali-kali kalian menyekutukan-Nya. Dan apa yang aku serukan kepada kalian ini adalah jalan menuju kebahagiaan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah adalah Rabbku dan Rabb kalian, maka sembahlah Dia) jika dibaca Anna maka dengan memperkirakan keberadaan lafal Udzkur, maksudnya: Ingatlah, sesungguhnya Allah dan seterusnya. Jika dibaca Kasrah yaitu Inna maka dengan memperkirakan keberadaan lafal Qul sebelumnya, maksudnya: Katakanlah, sesungguhnya Allah; hal ini dibuktikan oleh firman lainnya, yaitu: Aku tidak pernah mengatakan kepada mereka melainkan apa yang Engkau perintahkan kepadaku untuk mengatakannya, yaitu, "Sembahlah Allah, Rabbku dan Rabb kalian." (Q.S. Al-Maidah, 117). (ini) hal yang telah disebutkan tadi (adalah jalan) penuntun (yang lurus) yang dapat mengantarkan ke surga.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Allah adalah Tuhanku dan Tuhanmu, maka sembahIah Dia oleh kamu sekalian. Ini adalah jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (وأن الله ربي وربكم فاعبدوه) بفتح أن بتقدير اذكر، وبكسرها بتقدير قل بدليل "" ما قلت لهم إلا ما أمرتني به أن اعبدوا الله ربي وربكم "" (هذا) المذكور (صراط) طريق (مستقيم) مؤد إلى الجنة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَإِنَّ ٱللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمۡ فَٱعۡبُدُوهُۚ هَٰذَا صِرَٰطٞ مُّسۡتَقِيمٞ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Allah adalah Rabbku dan Rabb kalian, maka sembahlah Dia) jika dibaca Anna maka dengan memperkirakan keberadaan lafal Udzkur, maksudnya: Ingatlah, sesungguhnya Allah dan seterusnya. Jika dibaca Kasrah yaitu Inna maka dengan memperkirakan keberadaan lafal Qul sebelumnya, maksudnya: Katakanlah, sesungguhnya Allah; hal ini dibuktikan oleh firman lainnya, yaitu: Aku tidak pernah mengatakan kepada mereka melainkan apa yang Engkau perintahkan kepadaku untuk mengatakannya, yaitu, "Sembahlah Allah, Rabbku dan Rabb kalian." (Q.S. Al-Maidah, 117). (ini) hal yang telah disebutkan tadi (adalah jalan) penuntun (yang lurus) yang dapat mengantarkan ke surga

ar: فَٱخۡتَلَفَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ مِنۢ بَیۡنِهِمۡۖ فَوَیۡلࣱ لِّلَّذِینَ كَفَرُوا۟ مِن مَّشۡهَدِ یَوۡمٍ عَظِیمٍ
ar_simple: فَاخْتَلَفَ الْأَحْزَابُ مِنْ بَيْنِهِمْ ۖ فَوَيْلٌ لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ مَشْهَدِ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
transliteration: fa-ikh'talafa l-aḥzābu min baynihim fawaylun lilladhīna kafarū min mashhadi yawmin ʿaẓīmin

t.sahih-int: Then the factions differed [concerning Jesus] from among them, so woe to those who disbelieved - from the scene of a tremendous Day
t.asad: And yet, the sects [that follow the Bible] are at variance among themselves [about the nature of Jesus Woe, then, unto all who deny the truth when that awesome Day will appear]
t.yusuf-ali: But the sects differ among themselves: and woe to the unbelievers because of the (coming) Judgment of a Momentous Day
t.pickthall: The sects among them differ: but woe unto the disbelievers from the meeting of an awful Day
t.arberry: But the parties have fallen into variance among themselves; then woe to those who disbelieve for the scene of a dreadful day
t.hilali-khan: Then the sects differed [i.e. the Christians about 'Iesa (Jesus)], so woe unto the disbelievers [those who gave false witness by saying that 'Iesa (Jesus) is the son of Allah] from the meeting of a great Day (i.e. the Day of Resurrection, when they will be thrown in the blazing Fire)
t.itani: But the various factions differed among themselves. So woe to those who disbelieve from the scene of a tremendous Day.
t.mubarakpuri: Then the sects differed, so woe unto the disbelievers from the meeting of a great Day.
t.qarib: Then, the sects differed among themselves [concerning the nature of Jesus], so woe to those who reject the truth when they witness that awesome Day
t.wahiduddin: Yet different groups differed among themselves. How awful it will be for those who have rejected the truth when a dreadful Day arrives
t.shakir: But parties from among them disagreed with each other, so woe to those who disbelieve, because of presence on a great day
t.haleem: But factions have differed among themselves. What suffering will come to those who obscure the truth when a dreadful Day arrives
t.maariful: Then the groups among them fell in dispute. So, how evil is the fate of the disbelievers when they have to face the Great Day
t.khattab: Yet their ˹various˺ groups have differed among themselves ˹about him˺, so woe to the disbelievers when they face a tremendous Day
t.ghali: Yet the parties have differed among themselves; so woe to the ones who have disbelieved for the witnessing of a tremendous Day
t.irving: Factions have differed among themselves, yet how awful will it be for those who disbelieve when it comes to the spectacle on such a dreadful day
t.iwq: Then the factions differed concerning Jesus from among them, so woe to those who disbelieved - from the scene of a tremendous Day

t_id.kemenag: Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan (yang ada) di antara mereka. Maka kecelakaanlah bagi orang-orang kafir pada waktu menyaksikan hari yang besar.
t_id.sabiq: Meskipun begitu, orang-orang Ahl al-Kitâb berselisih paham tentang 'Isâ, dan terbagi-bagi menjadi beberapa kelompok. Maka, azab yang pedih disediakan bagi mereka saat mendatangi hari perhitungan, menyaksikan hari kiamat dan menerima balasan yang amat buruk.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan yang ada di antara mereka) yakni orang-orang Nasrani, yaitu sehubungan dengan perihal Isa, apakah dia anak Allah, atau tuhan di samping Allah, ataukah tuhan yang ketiga. (Maka kecelakaanlah) azab yang sangat keras (bagi orang-orang kafir) disebabkan apa yang telah disebutkan tadi dan hal-hal lainnya (pada waktu menyaksikan hari yang besar) yakni kehadiran mereka di hari kiamat dan kengerian-kengerian yang terjadi pada waktu itu.
t_id.iwq: Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan (yang ada) di antara mereka. Maka kecelakaanlah bagi orang-orang kafir pada waktu menyaksikan hari yang besar.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فاختلف الأحزاب من بينهم» أي النصارى في عيسى أهو ابن الله أو إله معه أو ثالث ثلاثة «فويل» فشدة عذاب «للذين كفروا» بما ذكر وغيره «من مشهد يوم عظيم» أي: حضور يوم القيامة وأهواله
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَٱخۡتَلَفَ ٱلۡأَحۡزَابُ مِنۢ بَيۡنِهِمۡۖ فَوَيۡلٞ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُواْ مِن مَّشۡهَدِ يَوۡمٍ عَظِيمٍ (الْأَحْزَابُ: الفِرَقُ مِنْ أَهْلِ الكِتَابِ, فَوَيْلٌ: فَهَلَاكٌ, مَّشْهَدِ: شُهُودِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Maka berselisihlah golongan-golongan yang ada di antara mereka) yakni orang-orang Nasrani, yaitu sehubungan dengan perihal Isa, apakah dia anak Allah, atau tuhan di samping Allah, ataukah tuhan yang ketiga. (Maka kecelakaanlah) azab yang sangat keras (bagi orang-orang kafir) disebabkan apa yang telah disebutkan tadi dan hal-hal lainnya (pada waktu menyaksikan hari yang besar) yakni kehadiran mereka di hari kiamat dan kengerian-kengerian yang terjadi pada waktu itu

ar: أَسۡمِعۡ بِهِمۡ وَأَبۡصِرۡ یَوۡمَ یَأۡتُونَنَاۖ لَـٰكِنِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ ٱلۡیَوۡمَ فِی ضَلَـٰلࣲ مُّبِینࣲ
ar_simple: أَسْمِعْ بِهِمْ وَأَبْصِرْ يَوْمَ يَأْتُونَنَا ۖ لَٰكِنِ الظَّالِمُونَ الْيَوْمَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُبِينٍ
transliteration: asmiʿ bihim wa-abṣir yawma yatūnanā lākini l-ẓālimūna l-yawma fī ḍalālin mubīnin

t.sahih-int: How [clearly] they will hear and see the Day they come to Us, but the wrongdoers today are in clear error
t.asad: How well will they hear and see [the truth] on the Day when they come before Us! Today, however, these evildoers are obviously lost in error
t.yusuf-ali: How plainly will they see and hear, the Day that they will appear before Us! but the unjust today are in error manifest
t.pickthall: See and hear them on the Day they come unto Us! yet the evil-doers are to-day in error manifest
t.arberry: How well they will hear and see on the day they come to Us! But the evildoers even today are in error manifest
t.hilali-khan: How clearly will they (polytheists and disbelievers in the Oneness of Allah) see and hear, the Day when they will appear before Us! But the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers) today are in plain error
t.itani: Listen to them and watch for them the Day they come to Us. But the wrongdoers today are completely lost.
t.mubarakpuri: How clearly will they see and hear, the Day when they will appear before Us! But the wrongdoers today are in plain error.
t.qarib: How [clearly] they will hear and see on the Day they will come before Us. Today, however, they are in clear error
t.wahiduddin: How sharp of hearing, how sharp of sight they will be when they come to Us. But today, these evil-doers are obviously lost in error
t.shakir: How clearly shall they hear and how clearly shall they see on the day when they come to Us; but the unjust this day are in manifest error
t.haleem: How sharp of hearing, how sharp of sight they will be when they come to Us, although now they are clearly off course! Warn them [Muhammad] of the Day of Remorse when the matter will be decided
t.maariful: How strong will their hearing be, and how strong their vision, the day they will come to Us! But today the transgressors are straying in clear error
t.khattab: How clearly will they hear and see on the Day they will come to Us! But today the wrongdoers are clearly astray
t.ghali: How well they will hear and behold the Day they come up to Us! But the unjust (ones) are today in evident error
t.irving: Listen to them and watch for the day when they will come to Us; though wrongdoers are in obvious error even today
t.iwq: How clearly they will hear and see the Day they come to Us, but the wrongdoers today are in clear error

t_id.kemenag: Alangkah terangnya pendengaran mereka dan alangkah tajamnya penglihatan mereka pada hari mereka datang kepada Kami. Tetapi orang-orang yang zalim pada hari ini (di dunia) berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata.
t_id.sabiq: Alangkah terangnya pendengaran mereka dan alangkah tajamnya penglihatan mereka pada hari mereka bertemu dengan Allah! Tetapi pada hari ini, karena sikap mereka yang menzalimi diri sendiri dan tidak mau memanfaatkan pendengaran dan penglihatannya ketika di dunia, mereka benar-benar sesat dari kebenaran.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Alangkah terangnya pendengaran mereka dan alangkah tajamnya penglihatan mereka) kedua lafal ini merupakan Shighat atau ungkapan rasa takjub, maknanya sama dengan lafal Ma Asma'ahum dan Ma Absharahum (pada hari mereka datang kepada Kami) di akhirat kelak.
