(Tetapi orang-orang yang zalim) menurut ungkapan meletakkan isim Zhahir pada tempat isim Mudhmar' (pada hari ini) yakni di dunia (berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata) nyata kesesatannya, disebabkan mereka tuli tidak mau mendengarkan perkara yang hak, dan mereka buta tidak mau melihat yang benar. Maksudnya, hai orang yang diajak bicara sepatutnya kamu merasa takjub dan heran terhadap pendengaran dan penglihatan mereka di akhirat, sesudah di dunia mereka tuli dan buta.
t_id.iwq: Alangkah terangnya pendengaran mereka dan alangkah tajamnya penglihatan mereka pada hari mereka datang kepada Kami. Tetapi orang-orang yang zalim pada hari ini (di dunia) berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «أسمع بهم وأبصر» بهم صيغتا تعجب بمعنى ما أسمعهم وما أبصرهم «يوم يأتوننا» في الآخرة «لكن الظالمون» من إقامة الظاهر مقام المضمر «اليوم» أي: في الدنيا «في ضلال مبين» أي بين به صموا عن سماع الحق وعموا عن إبصاره أي: اعجب منهم يا مخاطب في سمعهم وإبصارهم في الآخرة بعد أن كانوا في الدنيا صما عميا
tafsir.siraj-ar: أَسۡمِعۡ بِهِمۡ وَأَبۡصِرۡ يَوۡمَ يَأۡتُونَنَا لَٰكِنِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ ٱلۡيَوۡمَ فِي ضَلَٰلٖ مُّبِينٖ (أَسْمِعْ بِهِمْ وَأَبْصِرْ: مَا أَشَدَّ سَمْعَهُمْ وَبَصَرَهُمْ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Alangkah terangnya pendengaran mereka dan alangkah tajamnya penglihatan mereka) kedua lafal ini merupakan Shighat atau ungkapan rasa takjub, maknanya sama dengan lafal Ma Asma'ahum dan Ma Absharahum (pada hari mereka datang kepada Kami) di akhirat kelak. (Tetapi orang-orang yang zalim) menurut ungkapan meletakkan isim Zhahir pada tempat isim Mudhmar' (pada hari ini) yakni di dunia (berada dalam kesesatan yang nyata) nyata kesesatannya, disebabkan mereka tuli tidak mau mendengarkan perkara yang hak, dan mereka buta tidak mau melihat yang benar. Maksudnya, hai orang yang diajak bicara sepatutnya kamu merasa takjub dan heran terhadap pendengaran dan penglihatan mereka di akhirat, sesudah di dunia mereka tuli dan buta

ar: وَأَنذِرۡهُمۡ یَوۡمَ ٱلۡحَسۡرَةِ إِذۡ قُضِیَ ٱلۡأَمۡرُ وَهُمۡ فِی غَفۡلَةࣲ وَهُمۡ لَا یُؤۡمِنُونَ
ar_simple: وَأَنْذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْحَسْرَةِ إِذْ قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ وَهُمْ فِي غَفْلَةٍ وَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
transliteration: wa-andhir'hum yawma l-ḥasrati idh quḍiya l-amru wahum fī ghaflatin wahum lā yu'minūna

t.sahih-int: And warn them, [O Muhammad], of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded; and [yet], they are in [a state of] heedlessness, and they do not believe
t.asad: hence, warn them of [the coming of] the Day of Regrets, when everything will have been decided-for as yet they are heedless, and they do not believe [in it]
t.yusuf-ali: But warn them of the Day of Distress, when the matter will be determined: for (behold,) they are negligent and they do not believe
t.pickthall: And warn them of the Day of anguish when the case hath been decided. Now they are in a state of carelessness, and they believe not
t.arberry: Warn thou them of the day of anguish, when the matter shall be determined, and they yet heedless and unbelieving
t.hilali-khan: And warn them (O Muhammad SAW) of the Day of grief and regrets, when the case has been decided, while (now) they are in a state of carelessness, and they believe not
t.itani: And warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded. Yet they are heedless, and they do not believe.
t.mubarakpuri: And warn them of the Day of grief and regrets, when the case has been decided, while (now) they are in a state of carelessness, and they believe not.
t.qarib: Warn them of the Day of Regret, when everything will have been decided; and [yet], they are heedless, and they do not believe
t.wahiduddin: Warn them of [the coming of] the Day of Remorse, when everything will have been decided, while they are heedless and do not believe
t.shakir: And warn them of the day of intense regret, when the matter shall have been decided; and they are (now) in negligence and they do not believe
t.haleem: for they are heedless and do not believe
t.maariful: Warn them of The Day of Remorse when the whole thing will be settled, while they are heedless and do not believe
t.khattab: And warn them ˹O Prophet˺ of the Day of Regret, when all matters will be settled, while they are ˹engrossed˺ in heedlessness and disbelief
t.ghali: And warn them of the Day of regret, as the Command is decreed, and they are in a state of heedlessness, and they do not believe
t.irving: Warn them of the Day of Regret when the matter will be decided; they act so heedless and still do not believe
t.iwq: And warn them, O Muhammad, of the Day of Regret, when the matter will be concluded; and yet, they are in a state of heedlessness, and they do not believe

t_id.kemenag: Dan berilah mereka peringatan tentang hari penyesalan, (yaitu) ketika segala perkara telah diputus. Dan mereka dalam kelalaian dan mereka tidak (pula) beriman.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Rasul, berilah peringatan kepada orang-orang yang zalim tentang suatu hari tatkala mereka menyesal atas kelalaian mereka terhadap hak Allah dan hak mereka sendiri, sementara pada saat itu perkara mereka telah diputuskan dan sudah menerima balasan. Sesungguhnya mereka di dunia telah lalai dengan hari itu, karena mereka tidak percaya akan hari kebangkitan dan pembalasan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan berilah mereka peringatan) takut-takutilah hai Muhammad, orang-orang kafir Mekah itu (tentang hari penyesalan) yaitu hari kiamat sewaktu orang yang berbuat jahat merasa menyesal sekali karena tidak mau berbuat kebaikan di dunia (yaitu ketika segala perkara telah diputus) bagi mereka di hari kiamat, yaitu mereka harus menerima azab (Dan mereka) di dunia (dalam kelalaian) tentang hari penyesalan itu (dan mereka tidak pula beriman) kepada adanya hari penyesalan itu.
t_id.iwq: Dan berilah mereka peringatan tentang hari penyesalan, (yaitu) ketika segala perkara telah diputus. Dan mereka dalam kelalaian dan mereka tidak (pula) beriman.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأنذرهم» خوّف يا محمد كفار مكة «يوم الحسرة» هو يوم القيامة يتحسر فيه المسيء على ترك الإحسان في الدنيا «إذ قُضي الأمر» لهم فيه بالعذاب «وهم» في الدنيا «في غفلة» عنه «وهم لا يؤمنون» به
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأَنذِرۡهُمۡ يَوۡمَ ٱلۡحَسۡرَةِ إِذۡ قُضِيَ ٱلۡأَمۡرُ وَهُمۡ فِي غَفۡلَةٖ وَهُمۡ لَا يُؤۡمِنُونَ (الْحَسْرَةِ: النَّدَامَةِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan berilah mereka peringatan) takut-takutilah hai Muhammad, orang-orang kafir Mekah itu (tentang hari penyesalan) yaitu hari kiamat sewaktu orang yang berbuat jahat merasa menyesal sekali karena tidak mau berbuat kebaikan di dunia (yaitu ketika segala perkara telah diputus) bagi mereka di hari kiamat, yaitu mereka harus menerima azab (Dan mereka) di dunia (dalam kelalaian) tentang hari penyesalan itu (dan mereka tidak pula beriman) kepada adanya hari penyesalan itu

ar: إِنَّا نَحۡنُ نَرِثُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَنۡ عَلَیۡهَا وَإِلَیۡنَا یُرۡجَعُونَ
ar_simple: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَرِثُ الْأَرْضَ وَمَنْ عَلَيْهَا وَإِلَيْنَا يُرْجَعُونَ
transliteration: innā naḥnu narithu l-arḍa waman ʿalayhā wa-ilaynā yur'jaʿūna

t.sahih-int: Indeed, it is We who will inherit the earth and whoever is on it, and to Us they will be returned
t.asad: Behold, We alone shall remain after the earth and all who live on it have passed away, and (when] unto Us all will have been brought back
t.yusuf-ali: It is We Who will inherit the earth, and all beings thereon: to Us will they all be returned
t.pickthall: Lo! We, only We, inherit the earth and all who are thereon, and unto Us they are returned
t.arberry: Surely We shall inherit the earth and all that are upon it, and unto Us they shall be returned
t.hilali-khan: Verily! We will inherit the earth and whatsoever is thereon. And to Us they all shall be returned
t.itani: It is We who will inherit the earth and everyone on it, and to Us they will be returned.
t.mubarakpuri: Verily, We will inherit the earth and whatsoever is thereon. And to Us they all shall be returned.
t.qarib: It is We who will inherit the earth and all those on it, and to Us they will be returned
t.wahiduddin: It is We who will inherit the earth and all who dwell upon it: they shall all return to Us
t.shakir: Surely We inherit the earth and all those who are on it, and to Us they shall be returned
t.haleem: It is We who will inherit the earth and all who are on it: they will all be returned to Us
t.maariful: Surely We, only We, shall be the ultimate owner of the earth and all those on it, and to Us they will be returned
t.khattab: Indeed, it is We Who will succeed the earth and whoever is on it. And to Us they will ˹all˺ be returned
t.ghali: Surely, We, Ever We, will inherit the earth and (all) who are upon it, and to Us they will be returned
t.irving: It is We Who will inherit the earth plus anyone on it; to Us shall they return
t.iwq: Indeed, it is We who will inherit the earth and whoever is on it, and to Us they will be returned

t_id.kemenag: Sesungguhnya Kami mewarisi bumi dan semua orang-orang yang ada di atasnya, dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka dikembalikan.
t_id.sabiq: Ketahuilah wahai manusia, sesungguhnya Allahlah yang mewariskan alam dengan segala seisinya ini. Dan perhitungan untuk mereka ada pada Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami) lafal Nahnu berfungsi sebagai Taukid atau kata pengukuh (mewarisi bumi dan semua orang-orang yang ada di dalamnya) baik yang berakal maupun yang lainnya, yaitu dengan membinasakan mereka (dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka dikembalikan) pada hari kiamat untuk menerima pembalasan.
t_id.iwq: Sesungguhnya Kami mewarisi bumi dan semua orang-orang yang ada di atasnya, dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka dikembalikan.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إنا نحن» تأكيد «نرث الأرض ومن عليها» من العقلاء وغيرهم بإهلاكهم «وإلينا يرجعون» فيه للجزاء
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِنَّا نَحۡنُ نَرِثُ ٱلۡأَرۡضَ وَمَنۡ عَلَيۡهَا وَإِلَيۡنَا يُرۡجَعُونَ
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Sesungguhnya Kami) lafal Nahnu berfungsi sebagai Taukid atau kata pengukuh (mewarisi bumi dan semua orang-orang yang ada di dalamnya) baik yang berakal maupun yang lainnya, yaitu dengan membinasakan mereka (dan hanya kepada Kamilah mereka dikembalikan) pada hari kiamat untuk menerima pembalasan

ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِی ٱلۡكِتَـٰبِ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّیقࣰا نَّبِیًّا
ar_simple: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ صِدِّيقًا نَبِيًّا
transliteration: wa-udh'kur fī l-kitābi ib'rāhīma innahu kāna ṣiddīqan nabiyyan

t.sahih-int: And mention in the Book [the story of] Abraham. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet
t.asad: AND CALL to mind, through this divine writ, Abraham. Behold, he was a man of truth, [already] a prophet
t.yusuf-ali: Also mention in the Book (the story of) Abraham: He was a man of Truth, a prophet
t.pickthall: And make mention (O Muhammad) in the Scripture of Abraham. Lo! he was a saint, a prophet
t.arberry: And mention in the Book Abraham; surely he was a true man, a Prophet
t.hilali-khan: And mention in the Book (the Quran) Ibrahim (Abraham). Verily! He was a man of truth, a Prophet
t.itani: And mention in the Scripture Abraham. He was a man of truth, a prophet.
t.mubarakpuri: And mention in the Book, Ibrahim. Verily, he was a man of truth, a Prophet.
t.qarib: And mention in the Book [the story of] Abraham. He was a man of truth and a prophet
t.wahiduddin: Also recount the story of Abraham in the Book. He was a man of truth, and a prophet
t.shakir: And mention Ibrahim in the Book; surely he was a truthful man, a prophet
t.haleem: Mention too, in the Quran, the story of Abraham. He was a man of truth, a prophet
t.maariful: And mention in the Book (the story of) Ibrāhīm. Indeed, he was Siddīq (a man of truth), a prophet
t.khattab: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Abraham. He was surely a man of truth and a prophet
t.ghali: And mention in the Book Ibrahim; (Abraham) surely he was most sincere, a Prophet
t.irving: Mention Abraham in the Book. He was a truthful prophet
t.iwq: And mention in the Book the story of Abraham. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet

t_id.kemenag: Ceritakanlah (Hai Muhammad) kisah Ibrahim di dalam Al Kitab (Al Quran) ini. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan lagi seorang Nabi.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Rasul, ceritakanlah kepada manusia kisah Ibrâhîm yang terdapat dalam al-Qur'ân. Ibrâhîm sungguh seorang yang sangat benar perkataan dan perbuatannya dan seorang yang mendapatkan wahyu dari Allah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah) kepada mereka (tentang Ibrahim di dalam Al-Kitab ini) Alquran, yaitu tentang kisahnya. (Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan) seorang yang sangat jujur dalam keimanannya (lagi seorang nabi) hal ini dijelaskan dalam ayat selanjutnya.
t_id.iwq: Ceritakanlah (Hai Muhammad) kisah Ibrahim di dalam Al Kitab (Al Al-Quran) ini. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan lagi seorang Nabi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «واذكر» لهم «في الكتاب إبراهيم» أي: خبره «إنه كان صديقا» مبالغا في الصدق «نبيا» ويبدل من خبره
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ إِبۡرَٰهِيمَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّيقٗا نَّبِيًّا (صِدِّيقًا: عَظِيمَ الصِّدْقِ لَا يَكْذِبُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah) kepada mereka (tentang Ibrahim di dalam Al-Kitab ini) Alquran, yaitu tentang kisahnya. (Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan) seorang yang sangat jujur dalam keimanannya (lagi seorang nabi) hal ini dijelaskan dalam ayat selanjutnya

ar: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِیهِ یَـٰۤأَبَتِ لِمَ تَعۡبُدُ مَا لَا یَسۡمَعُ وَلَا یُبۡصِرُ وَلَا یُغۡنِی عَنكَ شَیۡءࣰا
ar_simple: إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ يَا أَبَتِ لِمَ تَعْبُدُ مَا لَا يَسْمَعُ وَلَا يُبْصِرُ وَلَا يُغْنِي عَنْكَ شَيْئًا
transliteration: idh qāla li-abīhi yāabati lima taʿbudu mā lā yasmaʿu walā yub'ṣiru walā yugh'nī ʿanka shayan

t.sahih-int: [Mention] when he said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all
t.asad: when he spoke [thus] unto his father: "O my father! Why dost thou worship something that neither hears nor sees and can be of no avail whatever to thee
t.yusuf-ali: Behold, he said to his father: "O my father! why worship that which heareth not and seeth not, and can profit thee nothing
t.pickthall: When he said unto his father: O my father! Why worshippest thou that which heareth not nor seeth, nor can in aught avail thee
t.arberry: When he said to his father, 'Father, why worshippest thou that which neither hears nor sees, nor avails thee anything
t.hilali-khan: When he said to his father: "O my father! Why do you worship that which hears not, sees not and cannot avail you in anything
t.itani: He said to his father, “O my father, why do you worship what can neither hear, nor see, nor benefit you in any way?
t.mubarakpuri: When he said to his father: "O my father! Why do you worship that which hears not, sees not and cannot avail you in anything"
t.qarib: He said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all
t.wahiduddin: He said to his father, "Why do you worship something that can neither hear nor see nor benefit you in any way
t.shakir: When he said to his father; O my father! why do you worship what neither hears nor sees, nor does it avail you in the least
t.haleem: He said to his father, ‘Father, why do you worship something that can neither hear nor see nor benefit you in any way
t.maariful: when he said to his father, “My dear father, why do you worship something that does neither hear nor see, nor help you in any way
t.khattab: ˹Remember˺ when he said to his father, “O dear father! Why do you worship what can neither hear nor see, nor benefit you at all
t.ghali: As he said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which neither hears nor beholds, nor avails you anything
t.irving: when he told his father: "My father, why do you worship something that neither hears nor perceives [anything], and does not benefit you in anyway
t.iwq: Mention when he said to his father, "O my father, why do you worship that which does not hear and does not see and will not benefit you at all

t_id.kemenag: Ingatlah ketika ia berkata kepada bapaknya; "Wahai bapakku, mengapa kamu menyembah sesuatu yang tidak mendengar, tidak melihat dan tidak dapat menolong kamu sedikitpun?
t_id.sabiq: Ingatlah ketika Ibrâhîm berkata dengan lemah lembut kepada bapaknya, "Wahai Bapak, mengapa engkau menyembah berhala yang tidak dapat mendengar dan melihat, juga tidak dapat mendatangkan kebaikan untukmu dan tidak dapat menolak kejahatan dari dirimu?
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika ia berkata kepada bapaknya) yang bernama Azar, ("Wahai bapakku!) huruf Ta pada lafal Abati ganti dari Ya Idhafah, karena keduanya tidak dapat dikumpulkan menjadi satu. Azar adalah penyembah berhala (Mengapa kamu menyembah sesuatu yang tidak mendengar, tidak melihat dan tidak dapat menolong kamu) tidak dapat mencukupimu (sedikit pun?) baik berupa manfaat maupun bahaya.
t_id.iwq: Ingatlah ketika ia berkata kepada bapaknya; "Wahai bapakku, mengapa kamu menyembah sesuatu yang tidak mendengar, tidak melihat dan tidak dapat menolong kamu sedikitpun?

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «إذ قال لأبيه» آزر «يا أبت» التاء عوض عن ياء الإضافة ولا يجمع بينهما وكان يعبد الأصنام «لمَ تعبد مالا يسمع ولا يبصر ولا يغني عنك» لا يكفيك «شيئا» من نفع أو ضر
tafsir.siraj-ar: إِذۡ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ يَـٰٓأَبَتِ لِمَ تَعۡبُدُ مَا لَا يَسۡمَعُ وَلَا يُبۡصِرُ وَلَا يُغۡنِي عَنكَ شَيۡـٔٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Yaitu ketika ia berkata kepada bapaknya) yang bernama Azar, ("Wahai bapakku!) huruf Ta pada lafal Abati ganti dari Ya Idhafah, karena keduanya tidak dapat dikumpulkan menjadi satu. Azar adalah penyembah berhala (Mengapa kamu menyembah sesuatu yang tidak mendengar, tidak melihat dan tidak dapat menolong kamu) tidak dapat mencukupimu (sedikit pun?) baik berupa manfaat maupun bahaya

ar: یَـٰۤأَبَتِ إِنِّی قَدۡ جَاۤءَنِی مِنَ ٱلۡعِلۡمِ مَا لَمۡ یَأۡتِكَ فَٱتَّبِعۡنِیۤ أَهۡدِكَ صِرَ ٰطࣰا سَوِیࣰّا
ar_simple: يَا أَبَتِ إِنِّي قَدْ جَاءَنِي مِنَ الْعِلْمِ مَا لَمْ يَأْتِكَ فَاتَّبِعْنِي أَهْدِكَ صِرَاطًا سَوِيًّا
transliteration: yāabati innī qad jāanī mina l-ʿil'mi mā lam yatika fa-ittabiʿ'nī ahdika ṣirāṭan sawiyyan

t.sahih-int: O my father, indeed there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you, so follow me; I will guide you to an even path
t.asad: O my father! Behold, there has indeed come to me [a ray] of knowledge such as has never yet come unto thee: follow me, then; I shall guide thee onto a perfect way
t.yusuf-ali: O my father! to me hath come knowledge which hath not reached thee: so follow me: I will guide thee to a way that is even and straight
t.pickthall: O my father! Lo! there hath come unto me of knowledge that which came not unto thee. So follow me, and I will lead thee on a right path
t.arberry: Father, there has come to me knowledge such as came not to thee; so follow me, and I will guide thee on a level path
t.hilali-khan: O my father! Verily! There has come to me of knowledge that which came not unto you. So follow me. I will guide you to a Straight Path
t.itani: O my father, there has come to me knowledge that never came to you. So follow me, and I will guide you along a straight way.
t.mubarakpuri: "O my father! Verily, there has come to me the knowledge of that which came not unto you. So follow me, I will guide you to the straight path."
t.qarib: Father, I have received knowledge which has not come to you, so follow me; I will guide you to an even path
t.wahiduddin: Father, I have been given some knowledge which has not come to you, so follow me: I shall guide you along a straight path
t.shakir: O my father! truly the knowledge has come to me which has not come to you, therefore follow me, I will guide you on a right path
t.haleem: Father, knowledge that has not reached you has come to me, so follow me: I will guide you to an even path
t.maariful: My dear father, indeed there has come to me a knowledge that has not come to you; so, follow me, and I shall lead you to a straight path
t.khattab: O dear father! I have certainly received some knowledge which you have not received, so follow me and I will guide you to the Straight Path
t.ghali: O my father, surely there has already come to me (something) of knowledge that did not come up to you; so closely follow me, and I will guide you on a level path
t.irving: My father, I have been given some knowledge which has not come to you, so follow me: I´ll guide you along a Level Road
t.iwq: O my father, verily there has come to me of knowledge that which has not come to you, so follow me; I will guide you to an even path

t_id.kemenag: Wahai bapakku, sesungguhnya telah datang kepadaku sebahagian ilmu pengetahuan yang tidak datang kepadamu, maka ikutilah aku, niscaya aku akan menunjukkan kepadamu jalan yang lurus.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Bapak, sesungguhnya aku telah memperoleh ilmu pengetahuan tentang Allah melalui wahyu yang tidak kamu peroleh, juga pengetahuan tentang kewajiban manusia terhadap Tuhannya. Oleh karena itu, ikutilah aku dan berimanlah kepada apa yang aku serukan kepadamu, niscaya aku akan tunjukkan kepadamu jalan lurus yang membawamu kepada kebenaran dan kebahagiaan.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Wahai bapakku! Sesungguhnya telah datang kepadaku sebagian ilmu pengetahuan yang tidak datang kepadamu, maka ikutilah aku, niscaya aku akan menunjukkan kepadamu jalan) penuntun (yang lurus) tidak menyimpang dari kebenaran.
t_id.iwq: Wahai bapakku, sesungguhnya telah datang kepadaku sebahagian ilmu pengetahuan yang tidak datang kepadamu, maka ikutilah aku, niscaya aku akan menunjukkan kepadamu jalan yang lurus.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا أبت إني قد جاءني من العلم ما لم يأتك فاتبعني أهدك صراطا» طريقا «سويا» مستقيما
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَـٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّي قَدۡ جَآءَنِي مِنَ ٱلۡعِلۡمِ مَا لَمۡ يَأۡتِكَ فَٱتَّبِعۡنِيٓ أَهۡدِكَ صِرَٰطٗا سَوِيّٗا (صِرَاطًا سَوِيًّا: طَرِيقًا لَا عِوَجَ فِيهِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Wahai bapakku! Sesungguhnya telah datang kepadaku sebagian ilmu pengetahuan yang tidak datang kepadamu, maka ikutilah aku, niscaya aku akan menunjukkan kepadamu jalan) penuntun (yang lurus) tidak menyimpang dari kebenaran

ar: یَـٰۤأَبَتِ لَا تَعۡبُدِ ٱلشَّیۡطَـٰنَۖ إِنَّ ٱلشَّیۡطَـٰنَ كَانَ لِلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ عَصِیࣰّا
ar_simple: يَا أَبَتِ لَا تَعْبُدِ الشَّيْطَانَ ۖ إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ كَانَ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ عَصِيًّا
transliteration: yāabati lā taʿbudi l-shayṭāna inna l-shayṭāna kāna lilrraḥmāni ʿaṣiyyan

t.sahih-int: O my father, do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan has ever been, to the Most Merciful, disobedient
t.asad: O my father! Do not worship Satan-for, verily, Satan is a rebel against the Most Gracious
t.yusuf-ali: O my father! serve not Satan: for Satan is a rebel against (Allah) Most Gracious
t.pickthall: O my father! Serve not the devil. Lo! the devil is a rebel unto the Beneficent
t.arberry: Father, serve not Satan; surely Satan is a rebel against the All-merciful
t.hilali-khan: O my father! Worship not Shaitan (Satan). Verily! Shaitan (Satan) has been a rebel against the Most Beneficent (Allah)
t.itani: O my father, do not worship the devil. The devil is disobedient to the Most Gracious.
t.mubarakpuri: "O my father! Worship not Shaytan. Verily, Shaytan has been a rebel against the Most Gracious."
t.qarib: O my father, do not worship Satan. Satan was disobedient to the Merciful
t.wahiduddin: Father! Do not worship Satan -- for, truly, Satan is a rebel against the Most Gracious One
t.shakir: O my father! serve not the Shaitan, surely the Shaitan is disobedient to the Beneficent Allah
t.haleem: Father, do not worship Satan- Satan has rebelled against the Lord of Mercy
t.maariful: My dear father, do not worship the Satan. The Satan is surely rebellious to the All-Merciful, (Allah)
t.khattab: O dear father! Do not worship Satan. Surely Satan is ever rebellious against the Most Compassionate
t.ghali: O my father, do not worship Ash-Shaytan; (The ever-vicious, i.e., the Devil) surely Ash-Shaytan has been most disobedient to The All-Merciful
t.irving: My father, do not serve Satan! Satan was defiant towards the Mercygiving
t.iwq: O my father, do not worship Satan. Indeed Satan has ever been, to the Ever-Merciful, disobedient

t_id.kemenag: Wahai bapakku, janganlah kamu menyembah syaitan. Sesungguhnya syaitan itu durhaka kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Bapak, janganlah engkau menaati setan untuk menyembah berhala seperti yang diajaknya kepadamu. Sesungguhnya setan itu selalu mendurhakai Allah dan menentang perintah-Nya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Wahai bapakku! Janganlah kamu menyembah setan) dengan ketaatanmu kepadanya, yaitu menyembah berhala. (Sesungguhnya setan itu durhaka kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) yang banyak durhakanya.
t_id.iwq: Wahai bapakku, janganlah kamu menyembah syaitan. Sesungguhnya syaitan itu durhaka kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا أبت لا تعبد الشيطان» بطاعتك إياه في عبادة الأصنام «إن الشيطان كان للرحمن عصيا» كثير العصيان
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَـٰٓأَبَتِ لَا تَعۡبُدِ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنَۖ إِنَّ ٱلشَّيۡطَٰنَ كَانَ لِلرَّحۡمَٰنِ عَصِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Wahai bapakku! Janganlah kamu menyembah setan) dengan ketaatanmu kepadanya, yaitu menyembah berhala. (Sesungguhnya setan itu durhaka kepada Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) yang banyak durhakanya

ar: یَـٰۤأَبَتِ إِنِّیۤ أَخَافُ أَن یَمَسَّكَ عَذَابࣱ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ فَتَكُونَ لِلشَّیۡطَـٰنِ وَلِیࣰّا
ar_simple: يَا أَبَتِ إِنِّي أَخَافُ أَنْ يَمَسَّكَ عَذَابٌ مِنَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ فَتَكُونَ لِلشَّيْطَانِ وَلِيًّا
transliteration: yāabati innī akhāfu an yamassaka ʿadhābun mina l-raḥmāni fatakūna lilshayṭāni waliyyan

t.sahih-int: O my father, indeed I fear that there will touch you a punishment from the Most Merciful so you would be to Satan a companion [in Hellfire]
t.asad: O my father! I dread lest a chastisement from the Most Gracious befall thee, and then thou wilt become [aware of having been] close unto Satan
t.yusuf-ali: O my father! I fear lest a Penalty afflict thee from (Allah) Most Gracious, so that thou become to Satan a friend
t.pickthall: O my father! Lo! I fear lest a punishment from the Beneficent overtake thee so that thou become a comrade of the devil
t.arberry: Father, I fear that some chastisement from the All-merciful will smite thee, so that thou becomest a friend to Satan
t.hilali-khan: O my father! Verily! I fear lest a torment from the Most Beneficent (Allah) overtake you, so that you become a companion of Shaitan (Satan) (in the Hell-fire)." [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi]
t.itani: O my father, I fear that a punishment from the Most Gracious will afflict you, and you become an ally of the devil.”
t.mubarakpuri: "O my father! Verily, I fear lest a torment from the Most Gracious should overtake you, so that you become a companion of Shaytan."
t.qarib: Father, I fear that a punishment from the Merciful will afflict you so that you will be Satan's companion [in Hell]
t.wahiduddin: Father, indeed I fear lest a punishment from the Gracious One afflict you, and you become a friend of Satan
t.shakir: O my father! surely I fear that a punishment from the Beneficent Allah should afflict you so that you should be a friend of the Shaitan
t.haleem: Father, I fear that a punishment from the Lord of Mercy may afflict you and that you may become Satan’s companion [in Hell].’
t.maariful: My dear father, I fear lest a punishment from the All- Merciful (Allah) should afflict you, and you become a companion of Satan.”
t.khattab: O dear father! I truly fear that you will be touched by a torment from the Most Compassionate, and become Satan’s companion ˹in Hell˺.”
t.ghali: O my Father, surely I fear that (some) torment from The All-Merciful will touch you, so that you become a patron to Ash-Shaytan
t.irving: My father, I fear lest some torment from the Mercy-giving should afflict you, and you become a partisan of Satan
t.iwq: O my father, indeed I fear that there will touch you a punishment from the Ever-Merciful so you would be to Satan a companion in Hellfire

t_id.kemenag: Wahai bapakku, sesungguhnya aku khawatir bahwa kamu akan ditimpa azab dari Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka kamu menjadi kawan bagi syaitan".
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Bapak, sesungguhnya aku khawatir jika kamu terus berada dalam kekufuran, kamu akan ditimpa azab yang sangat pedih dari Allah, sehingga menjadi teman setan, yang saling menolong, di dalam neraka."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Wahai bapakku! Sesungguhnya aku khawatir bahwa kamu akan ditimpa azab dari Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) jika kamu tidak bertobat (maka kamu menjadi kawan bagi setan)" yaitu menjadi penolong dan temannya di neraka.
t_id.iwq: Wahai bapakku, sesungguhnya aku khawatir bahwa kamu akan ditimpa azab dari Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah, maka kamu menjadi kawan bagi syaitan".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «يا أبت إني أخاف أن يمسك عذاب من الرحمن» إن لم تتب «فتكون للشيطان وليا» ناصرا وقرينا في النار
tafsir.siraj-ar: يَـٰٓأَبَتِ إِنِّيٓ أَخَافُ أَن يَمَسَّكَ عَذَابٞ مِّنَ ٱلرَّحۡمَٰنِ فَتَكُونَ لِلشَّيۡطَٰنِ وَلِيّٗا (وَلِيًّا: قَرِينًا فِي النَّارِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Wahai bapakku! Sesungguhnya aku khawatir bahwa kamu akan ditimpa azab dari Tuhan Yang Maha Pemurah) jika kamu tidak bertobat (maka kamu menjadi kawan bagi setan)" yaitu menjadi penolong dan temannya di neraka

ar: قَالَ أَرَاغِبٌ أَنتَ عَنۡ ءَالِهَتِی یَـٰۤإِبۡرَ ٰهِیمُۖ لَئِن لَّمۡ تَنتَهِ لَأَرۡجُمَنَّكَۖ وَٱهۡجُرۡنِی مَلِیࣰّا
ar_simple: قَالَ أَرَاغِبٌ أَنْتَ عَنْ آلِهَتِي يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ ۖ لَئِنْ لَمْ تَنْتَهِ لَأَرْجُمَنَّكَ ۖ وَاهْجُرْنِي مَلِيًّا
transliteration: qāla arāghibun anta ʿan ālihatī yāib'rāhīmu la-in lam tantahi la-arjumannaka wa-uh'jur'nī maliyyan

t.sahih-int: [His father] said, "Have you no desire for my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will surely stone you, so avoid me a prolonged time
t.asad: He answered: "Dost thou dislike my gods, O Abraham? Indeed, if thou desist not, I shall most certainly cause thee to be stoned to death! Now begone from me for good
t.yusuf-ali: (The father) replied: "Dost thou hate my gods, O Abraham? If thou forbear not, I will indeed stone thee: Now get away from me for a good long while
t.pickthall: He said: Rejectest thou my gods, O Abraham? If thou cease not, I shall surely stone thee. Depart from me a long while
t.arberry: Said he, 'What, art thou shrinking from my gods, Abraham? Surely, if thou givest not over, I shall stone thee; so forsake me now for some while
t.hilali-khan: He (the father) said: "Do you reject my gods, O Ibrahim (Abraham)? If you stop not (this), I will indeed stone you. So get away from me safely before I punish you
t.itani: He said, “Are you renouncing my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will stone you. So leave me alone for a while.”
t.mubarakpuri: He (the father) said: "Do you revile my gods, O Ibrahim If you stop not (this), I will La'arjumannak. So get away from me Maliyan."
t.qarib: [His father] said, "Are you forsaking my gods, Abraham? If you do not stop this, I will surely stone you. Now get away from me and don't come back
t.wahiduddin: [His father] said, "Do you reject my deities, Abraham? If you do not desist, I shall surely stone you to death. Keep out of my way
t.shakir: He said: Do you dislike my gods, O Ibrahim? If you do not desist I will certainly revile you, and leave me for a time
t.haleem: His father answered, ‘Abraham, do you reject my gods? I will stone you if you do not stop this. Keep out of my way!’
t.maariful: He said, “Are you averse to my gods O Ibrāhīm? Should you not stop, I will definitely stone you. And leave me for good.”
t.khattab: He threatened, “How dare you reject my idols, O Abraham! If you do not desist, I will certainly stone you ˹to death˺. So be gone from me for a long time!”
t.ghali: Said he, "Do you desirously shirk from my gods, O Ibrahim? Indeed, in case you do not refrain, indeed I will definitely stone you; so forsake me for a long while
t.irving: He said: "Do you dislike my gods, Abraham? If you do not stop, I´ll cast you out. Leave me alone as soon as you can
t.iwq: His father said, "Have you no desire for my gods, O Abraham? If you do not desist, I will surely stone you, so avoid me a prolonged time

t_id.kemenag: Berkata bapaknya: "Bencikah kamu kepada tuhan-tuhanku, hai Ibrahim? Jika kamu tidak berhenti, maka niscaya kamu akan kurajam, dan tinggalkanlah aku buat waktu yang lama".
t_id.sabiq: Dengan sikap ingkar, bapaknya berkata dan mengancam Ibrâhîm, "Wahai Ibrâhîm, mengapa kamu berpaling dari tuhan-tuhanku dan mengajakku untuk menyembah Tuhanmu? Jika kamu tidak berhenti mencela tuhanku, akan aku lempari kamu dengan bebatuan. Berhati-hatilah kamu terhadapku dan tinggalkanlah aku untuk waktu yang lama sampai reda amarahku kepadamu."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Bapaknya berkata, "Bencikah kamu kepada tuhan-tuhanku, hai Ibrahim?) maka sebab itu kamu mencelanya. (Jika kamu tidak berhenti) mencaci makinya (maka niscaya kamu akan kurajam) dengan batu, atau dengan perkataan yang jelek, maka hati-hatilah kamu terhadapku (dan tinggalkanlah aku buat waktu yang lama)" yakni dalam masa yang lama.
t_id.iwq: Berkata bapaknya: "Bencikah kamu kepada tuhan-tuhanku, hai Ibrahim? Jika kamu tidak berhenti, maka niscaya kamu akan kurajam, dan tinggalkanlah aku buat waktu yang lama".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «قال أراغب أنت عن آلهتي يا إبراهيم» فتعيبها «لئن لم تنته» عن التعرض لها «لأرجمنَّك» بالحجارة أو بالكلام القبيح فاحذرني «واهجرني مليّا» دهرا طويلاً
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ أَرَاغِبٌ أَنتَ عَنۡ ءَالِهَتِي يَـٰٓإِبۡرَٰهِيمُۖ لَئِن لَّمۡ تَنتَهِ لَأَرۡجُمَنَّكَۖ وَٱهۡجُرۡنِي مَلِيّٗا (مَلِيًّا: زَمَنًا طَوِيلًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Bapaknya berkata, "Bencikah kamu kepada tuhan-tuhanku, hai Ibrahim?) maka sebab itu kamu mencelanya. (Jika kamu tidak berhenti) mencaci makinya (maka niscaya kamu akan kurajam) dengan batu, atau dengan perkataan yang jelek, maka hati-hatilah kamu terhadapku (dan tinggalkanlah aku buat waktu yang lama)" yakni dalam masa yang lama

ar: قَالَ سَلَـٰمٌ عَلَیۡكَۖ سَأَسۡتَغۡفِرُ لَكَ رَبِّیۤۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِی حَفِیࣰّا
ar_simple: قَالَ سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكَ ۖ سَأَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكَ رَبِّي ۖ إِنَّهُ كَانَ بِي حَفِيًّا
transliteration: qāla salāmun ʿalayka sa-astaghfiru laka rabbī innahu kāna bī ḥafiyyan

t.sahih-int: [Abraham] said, "Peace will be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you of my Lord. Indeed, He is ever gracious to me
t.asad: [Abraham] replied: "Peace be upon thee! I shall ask my Sustainer to forgive thee: for, behold, He has always been kind unto me
t.yusuf-ali: Abraham said: "Peace be on thee: I will pray to my Lord for thy forgiveness: for He is to me Most Gracious
t.pickthall: He said: Peace be unto thee! I shall ask forgiveness of my Lord for thee. Lo! He was ever gracious unto me
t.arberry: He said, 'Peace be upon thee! I will ask my Lord to forgive thee; surely He is ever gracious to me
t.hilali-khan: Ibrahim (Abraham) said: "Peace be on you! I will ask Forgiveness of my Lord for you. Verily! He is unto me, Ever Most Gracious
t.itani: He said, “Peace be upon you. I will ask my Lord to forgive you; He has been Kind to me.
t.mubarakpuri: Ibrahim said: "Peace be on you! I will ask forgiveness of my Lord for you. Verily, He is unto me Hafiyyan.
t.qarib: He said, "Peace be upon you. I will ask my Lord to forgive you. He is always gracious to me
t.wahiduddin: Abraham replied, "Peace be on you: I will pray to my Lord for your forgiveness, He has indeed been gracious to me
t.shakir: He said: Peace be on you, I will pray to my Lord to forgive you; surely He is ever Affectionate to me
t.haleem: Abraham said, ‘Peace be with you: I will beg my Lord to forgive you- He is always gracious to me
t.maariful: He said, “Peace on you. I shall seek forgiveness for you from my Lord. He has always been gracious to me
t.khattab: Abraham responded, “Peace be upon you! I will pray to my Lord for your forgiveness. He has truly been Most Gracious to me
t.ghali: He said, "Peace be upon you! I will soon ask my Lord to forgive you; surely He has been Ever-Hospitable to me
t.irving: He said: "Peace be upon you! I´ll seek forgiveness for you from my Lord; He has been so Gracious towards me
t.iwq: Abraham said, "Peace will be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you of my Lord. Indeed, He is ever gracious to me

t_id.kemenag: Berkata Ibrahim: "Semoga keselamatan dilimpahkan kepadamu, aku akan memintakan ampun bagimu kepada Tuhanku. Sesungguhnya Dia sangat baik kepadaku.
t_id.sabiq: Ibrâhîm bersikap lembut terhadap bapaknya, lalu mengucapkan salam perpisahan seraya berkata, "Semoga keselamatan dilimpahkan kepadamu, dan aku akan memohon kepada Tuhanku agar engkau mendapatkan petunjuk dan ampunan. Sesungguhnya Tuhanku selalu menyayangiku dan berada di dekatku.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata, "Semoga keselamatan dilimpahkan kepadamu) dariku, maksudnya aku tidak akan lagi menimpakan hal-hal yang tidak diinginkan kepadamu (aku akan memintakan ampun bagimu kepada Rabbku. Sesungguhnya Dia sangat baik kepadaku") lafal Hafiyyan berasal dari lafal Hafaa, yang artinya sangat baik hingga Dia selalu memperkenankan doaku. Kemudian Nabi Ibrahim memenuhi janjinya itu, sebagaimana disebutkan di dalam surah Asy-Syu'ara, yaitu, ".. dan ampunilah bapakku..." (Q.S. Asy-Syu'ara, 86). Hal ini dilakukan oleh Nabi Ibrahim sebelum jelas baginya bahwa bapaknya itu adalah musuh Allah, sebagaimana yang telah disebutkan di dalam surah At-Taubah.
t_id.iwq: Berkata Ibrahim: "Semoga keselamatan dilimpahkan kepadamu, aku akan memintakan ampun bagimu kepada Tuhanku. Sesungguhnya Dia sangat baik kepadaku.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (قال سلام عليك) مني أي لا أصيبك بمكروه (سأستغفر لك ربي إنه كان بي حفيا) من حفي أي بارا فيجيب دعائي وقد أوفى بوعده المذكور في الشعراء "" واغفر لأبي "" وهذا قبل أن يتبين له أنه عدو الله كما ذكره في براءة
tafsir.siraj-ar: قَالَ سَلَٰمٌ عَلَيۡكَۖ سَأَسۡتَغۡفِرُ لَكَ رَبِّيٓۖ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ بِي حَفِيّٗا (حَفِيًّا: رَحِيمًا بِحَالِي يُكْرِمُنِي وَيُجِيبُنِي إِذَا دَعَوْتُهُ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ibrahim berkata, "Semoga keselamatan dilimpahkan kepadamu) dariku, maksudnya aku tidak akan lagi menimpakan hal-hal yang tidak diinginkan kepadamu (aku akan memintakan ampun bagimu kepada Rabbku. Sesungguhnya Dia sangat baik kepadaku") lafal Hafiyyan berasal dari lafal Hafaa, yang artinya sangat baik hingga Dia selalu memperkenankan doaku. Kemudian Nabi Ibrahim memenuhi janjinya itu, sebagaimana disebutkan di dalam surah Asy-Syu'ara, yaitu, ".. dan ampunilah bapakku..." (Q.S. Asy-Syu'ara, 86). Hal ini dilakukan oleh Nabi Ibrahim sebelum jelas baginya bahwa bapaknya itu adalah musuh Allah, sebagaimana yang telah disebutkan di dalam surah At-Taubah

ar: وَأَعۡتَزِلُكُمۡ وَمَا تَدۡعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَأَدۡعُوا۟ رَبِّی عَسَىٰۤ أَلَّاۤ أَكُونَ بِدُعَاۤءِ رَبِّی شَقِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَأَعْتَزِلُكُمْ وَمَا تَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ وَأَدْعُو رَبِّي عَسَىٰ أَلَّا أَكُونَ بِدُعَاءِ رَبِّي شَقِيًّا
transliteration: wa-aʿtazilukum wamā tadʿūna min dūni l-lahi wa-adʿū rabbī ʿasā allā akūna biduʿāi rabbī shaqiyyan

t.sahih-int: And I will leave you and those you invoke other than Allah and will invoke my Lord. I expect that I will not be in invocation to my Lord unhappy
t.asad: But I shall withdraw from you all and from whatever you invoke instead of God, and shall invoke my Sustainer [alone]: it may well be that my prayer [for thee] will not remain unanswered by my Sustainer
t.yusuf-ali: And I will turn away from you (all) and from those whom ye invoke besides Allah: I will call on my Lord: perhaps, by my prayer to my Lord, I shall be not unblest
t.pickthall: I shall withdraw from you and that unto which ye pray beside Allah, and I shall pray unto my Lord. It may be that, in prayer unto my Lord, I shall not be unblest
t.arberry: Now I will go apart from you and that you call upon, apart from God; I will call upon my Lord, and haply I shall not be, in calling upon my Lord, unprosperous
t.hilali-khan: And I shall turn away from you and from those whom you invoke besides Allah. And I shall call on my Lord; and I hope that I shall not be unblest in my invocation to my Lord
t.itani: And I will withdraw from you, and from what you pray to instead of God. And I will pray to my Lord, and I hope I will not be disappointed in my prayer to my Lord.”
t.mubarakpuri: And I shall turn away from you and from those whom you invoke besides Allah. And I shall call upon my Lord, and I certainly hope that I shall not be unblessed in my invocation to my Lord."
t.qarib: and I will leave you and those you call upon other than God and will call upon my Lord. Perhaps in calling on my Lord, I will be blessed
t.wahiduddin: I will separate myself from you and from whatever you call upon besides God, and I will pray only to my Lord. It may well be that, in calling on my Lord, I will not be disappointed
t.shakir: And I will withdraw from you and what you call on besides Allah, and I will call upon my Lord; may be I shall not remain unblessed in calling upon my Lord
t.haleem: but for now I will leave you, and the idols you all pray to, and I will pray to my Lord and trust that my prayer will not be in vain.’
t.maariful: And I go away from you and what you invoke besides Allah. And I shall invoke my Lord. Hopefully I shall not be a looser by invoking my Lord.”
t.khattab: As I distance myself from ˹all of˺ you and from whatever you invoke besides Allah, I will ˹continue to˺ call upon my Lord ˹alone˺, trusting that I will never be disappointed in invoking my Lord.”
t.ghali: And I keep apart from you and what you invoke apart from Allah; and I will invoke my Lord. It may be that in invoking my Lord, I will not be wretched
t.irving: I´ll move away from you (all) and from anything you appeal to instead of God. I shall appeal to my Lord; perhaps I´ll not feel quite so miserable with my Lord´s appeal
t.iwq: And I will leave you and those you invoke other than Allah and will invoke my Lord. I expect that I will not be in invocation to my Lord unhappy

t_id.kemenag: Dan aku akan menjauhkan diri darimu dan dari apa yang kamu seru selain Allah, dan aku akan berdoa kepada Tuhanku, mudah-mudahan aku tidak akan kecewa dengan berdoa kepada Tuhanku".
t_id.sabiq: Kini tibalah saatnya aku meninggalkan kalian semua dan menjauhi apa yang kalian sembah selain Allah. Aku akan menyembah Tuhanku semata, dengan harapan Dia akan menerima ketaatanku dan tidak menyia- nyiakan harapanku."
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan aku akan menjauhkan diri daripada kalian dan daripada apa yang kalian seru) yang kalian sembah (selain Allah, dan aku akan berdoa) yakni aku akan menyembah (kepada Rabbku, mudah-mudahan aku dengan berdoa kepada Rabbku) dengan beribadah kepada-Nya (tidak akan kecewa)" sebagaimana kalian kecewa karena menyembah berhala-berhala itu.
t_id.iwq: Dan aku akan menjauhkan diri darimu dan dari apa yang kamu seru selain Allah, dan aku akan berdoa kepada Tuhanku, mudah-mudahan aku tidak akan kecewa dengan berdoa kepada Tuhanku".

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وأعتزلكم وما تدعون» تعبدون «من دون الله وأدعو» أعبد «ربي عسى أ» ن «لا أكون بدعاء ربي» بعبادته «شقيا» كما شقيتم بعبادة الأصنام
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَأَعۡتَزِلُكُمۡ وَمَا تَدۡعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَأَدۡعُواْ رَبِّي عَسَىٰٓ أَلَّآ أَكُونَ بِدُعَآءِ رَبِّي شَقِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan aku akan menjauhkan diri daripada kalian dan daripada apa yang kalian seru) yang kalian sembah (selain Allah, dan aku akan berdoa) yakni aku akan menyembah (kepada Rabbku, mudah-mudahan aku dengan berdoa kepada Rabbku) dengan beribadah kepada-Nya (tidak akan kecewa)" sebagaimana kalian kecewa karena menyembah berhala-berhala itu

ar: فَلَمَّا ٱعۡتَزَلَهُمۡ وَمَا یَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥۤ إِسۡحَـٰقَ وَیَعۡقُوبَۖ وَكُلࣰّا جَعَلۡنَا نَبِیࣰّا
ar_simple: فَلَمَّا اعْتَزَلَهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللَّهِ وَهَبْنَا لَهُ إِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ ۖ وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنَا نَبِيًّا
transliteration: falammā iʿ'tazalahum wamā yaʿbudūna min dūni l-lahi wahabnā lahu is'ḥāqa wayaʿqūba wakullan jaʿalnā nabiyyan

t.sahih-int: So when he had left them and those they worshipped other than Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each [of them] We made a prophet
t.asad: And after he had withdrawn from them and from all that they were worshipping instead of God, We bestowed upon him Isaac and Jacob, and made each of them a prophet
t.yusuf-ali: When he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and each one of them We made a prophet
t.pickthall: So, when he had withdrawn from them and that which they were worshipping beside Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob. Each of them We made a prophet
t.arberry: So, when he went apart from them and that they were serving, apart from God, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each We made a Prophet
t.hilali-khan: So when he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Ishaque (Isaac) and Ya'qub (Jacob), and each one of them We made a Prophet
t.itani: When he withdrew from them, and from what they worship besides God, We granted him Isaac and Jacob. And each We made a prophet.
t.mubarakpuri: So when he had turned away from them and from those whom they worshipped besides Allah, We gave him Ishaq and Ya`qub, and each one of them We made a Prophet.
t.qarib: So when he had left them and those they worshipped other than God, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each [of them] We made a prophet
t.wahiduddin: So when he had separated himself from them and from what they worshipped besides God, We bestowed on him Isaac and Jacob, and We made each of them a prophet
t.shakir: So when he withdrew from them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We gave to him Ishaq and Yaqoub, and each one of them We made a prophet
t.haleem: When he left his people and those they served beside God, We granted him Isaac and Jacob and made them both prophets
t.maariful: So, when he went away from them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We blessed him with IsHāq (Isaac) and Ya‘qūb (Jacob) and We made each one of them a prophet
t.khattab: So after he had left them and what they worshipped besides Allah, We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and made each of them a prophet
t.ghali: So, as soon as he kept apart from them and what they were worshiping apart from Allah, We bestowed upon him Ishaq and Yaaqub, (Isaac and Jacob, respectively) and each We made a Prophet
t.irving: When he moved away from them and what they worshipped instead of God, We bestowed Isaac and Jacob on him. Each We made a prophet
t.iwq: So when he had left them and those they worshipped other than Allah, We gave him Isaac and Jacob, and each of them We made a prophet

t_id.kemenag: Maka ketika Ibrahim sudah menjauhkan diri dari mereka dan dari apa yang mereka sembah selain Allah, Kami anugerahkan kepadanya Ishak, dan Ya'qub. Dan masing-masingnya Kami angkat menjadi nabi.
t_id.sabiq: Ketika Ibrâhîm telah memisahkan diri dari bapaknya, kaumnya, dan tuhan-tuhan mereka, Allah kemudian memuliakannya dengan memberikan kepadanya keturunan yang saleh, pada saat-saat ia hampir putus asa. Sebab, kala itu, ia dan istrinya telah sampai pada batas umur yang tidak mungkin lagi memperoleh keturunan. Allah menganugerahkan kepadanya Ishâq, dan dari Ishâq Allah memberi Ya'qûb yang semuanya dijadikan nabi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Ketika Ibrahim sudah menjauhkan diri dari mereka dan dari apa yang mereka sembah selain Allah) dia pergi ke tanah suci (Kami anugerahkan kepadanya) dua orang putra yang menjadi penghibur hatinya (Ishak dan Yakub. Dan masing-masingnya) di antara keduanya (Kami angkat menjadi nabi).
t_id.iwq: Maka ketika Ibrahim sudah menjauhkan diri dari mereka dan dari apa yang mereka sembah selain Allah, Kami anugerahkan kepadanya Ishak, dan Ya'qub. Dan masing-masingnya Kami angkat menjadi nabi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «فلما اعتزلهم وما يعبدون من دون الله» بأن ذهب إلى الأرض المقدسة «وهبنا له» ابنين يأنس بهما «إسحاق ويعقوب وكلا» منهما «جعلنا نبيا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: فَلَمَّا ٱعۡتَزَلَهُمۡ وَمَا يَعۡبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥٓ إِسۡحَٰقَ وَيَعۡقُوبَۖ وَكُلّٗا جَعَلۡنَا نَبِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Ketika Ibrahim sudah menjauhkan diri dari mereka dan dari apa yang mereka sembah selain Allah) dia pergi ke tanah suci (Kami anugerahkan kepadanya) dua orang putra yang menjadi penghibur hatinya (Ishak dan Yakub. Dan masing-masingnya) di antara keduanya (Kami angkat menjadi nabi)

ar: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُم مِّن رَّحۡمَتِنَا وَجَعَلۡنَا لَهُمۡ لِسَانَ صِدۡقٍ عَلِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُمْ مِنْ رَحْمَتِنَا وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ عَلِيًّا
transliteration: wawahabnā lahum min raḥmatinā wajaʿalnā lahum lisāna ṣid'qin ʿaliyyan

t.sahih-int: And We gave them of Our mercy, and we made for them a reputation of high honor
t.asad: and We bestowed upon them [manifold] gifts out of Our grace, and granted them a lofty power to convey the truth [unto others]
t.yusuf-ali: And We bestowed of Our Mercy on them, and We granted them lofty honour on the tongue of truth
t.pickthall: And we gave them of Our mercy, and assigned to them a high and true renown
t.arberry: and We gave them of Our mercy, and We appointed unto them a tongue of truthfulness, sublime
t.hilali-khan: And We gave them of Our Mercy (a good provision in plenty), and We granted them honour on the tongues (of all the nations, i.e. everybody remembers them with a good praise)
t.itani: And We gave them freely of Our mercy, and gave them a noble reputation of truth.
t.mubarakpuri: And We gave them of Our mercy, and We granted them Sidqin `Aliyyan on the tongues.
t.qarib: And We endowed them with Our mercy, and we made them highly honored
t.wahiduddin: We granted them Our mercy and bestowed on them true and high renown
t.shakir: And We granted to them of Our mercy, and We left (behind them) a truthful mention of eminence for them
t.haleem: We granted Our grace to all of them, and gave them a noble reputation
t.maariful: and We bestowed on them (a great deal) of our mercy, and caused them to have a good name at a high level
t.khattab: We showered them with Our mercy, and blessed them with honourable mention
t.ghali: And We bestowed upon them of Our mercy, and We made for them a tongue of sincerity, most exalted
t.irving: We bestowed some of Our mercy on them and granted them a sublime tongue for telling truth
t.iwq: And We gave them of Our mercy, and we made for them a reputation of high honor

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami anugerahkan kepada mereka sebagian dari rahmat Kami dan Kami jadikan mereka buah tutur yang baik lagi tinggi.
t_id.sabiq: Lebih dari kenabian, Kami pun memberikan mereka banyak kebaikan agama dan dunia dengan rahmat Kami. Kami memberikan pula kenangan baik yang abadi kepada mereka melalui buah tutur yang baik lagi terhormat yang selalu menyebut mereka.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami anugerahkan kepada mereka) bertiga Nabi Ibrahim, Nabi Ishak, dan Nabi Yakub (sebagian dari rahmat Kami,) berupa harta benda dan anak-anak (dan Kami jadikan mereka buah tutur yang baik lagi tinggi) mereka selalu menjadi pujian dan sanjungan semua pemeluk agama.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami anugerahkan kepada mereka sebagian dari rahmat Kami dan Kami jadikan mereka buah tutur yang baik lagi tinggi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ووهبنا لهم» للثلاثة «من رحمتنا» المال والولد «وجعلنا لهم لسان صدق عليا» رفيعا هو الثناء الحسن في جميع أهل الأديان
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُم مِّن رَّحۡمَتِنَا وَجَعَلۡنَا لَهُمۡ لِسَانَ صِدۡقٍ عَلِيّٗا (لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ عَلِيًّا: ذِكْرًا حَسَنًا، وَثَنَاءً بَاقِيًا فِي النَّاسِ)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami anugerahkan kepada mereka) bertiga Nabi Ibrahim, Nabi Ishak, dan Nabi Yakub (sebagian dari rahmat Kami,) berupa harta benda dan anak-anak (dan Kami jadikan mereka buah tutur yang baik lagi tinggi) mereka selalu menjadi pujian dan sanjungan semua pemeluk agama

ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِی ٱلۡكِتَـٰبِ مُوسَىٰۤۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مُخۡلَصࣰا وَكَانَ رَسُولࣰا نَّبِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ مُوسَىٰ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ مُخْلَصًا وَكَانَ رَسُولًا نَبِيًّا
transliteration: wa-udh'kur fī l-kitābi mūsā innahu kāna mukh'laṣan wakāna rasūlan nabiyyan

t.sahih-int: And mention in the Book, Moses. Indeed, he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet
t.asad: AND CALL to mind, through this divine writ, Moses. Behold, he was a chosen one, and was an apostle [of God], a prophet
t.yusuf-ali: Also mention in the Book (the story of) Moses: for he was specially chosen, and he was a messenger (and) a prophet
t.pickthall: And make mention in the Scripture of Moses. Lo! he was chosen, and he was a messenger (of Allah), a prophet
t.arberry: And mention in the Book Moses; he was devoted, and he was a Messenger, a Prophet
t.hilali-khan: And mention in the Book (this Quran) Musa (Moses). Verily! He was chosen and he was a Messenger (and) a Prophet
t.itani: And mention in the Scripture Moses. He was dedicated. He was a messenger and a prophet.
t.mubarakpuri: And mention in the Book, Musa. Verily, he was Mukhlasan and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet.
t.qarib: And mention in the Book, Moses. He was sincere, and he was a messenger and a prophet
t.wahiduddin: Tell also of Moses in the Book. He was indeed a chosen one, and was a messenger and a prophet
t.shakir: And mention Musa in the Book; surely he was one purified, and he was an apostle, a prophet
t.haleem: Mention too, in the Quran, the story of Moses. He was specially chosen, a messenger and a prophet
t.maariful: And mention in the Book (the story of) Mūsā. Indeed he was a chosen one and was a messenger, a prophet
t.khattab: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Moses. He was truly a chosen man, and was a messenger and a prophet
t.ghali: And mention in the Book Musa; (Moses) surely he was most faithful and he was a Messenger, a Prophet
t.irving: Mention Moses in the Book. He was sincere, and was a messenger, a prophet
t.iwq: And mention in the Book, Moses. Indeed, he was chosen, and he was a messenger and a prophet

t_id.kemenag: Dan ceritakanlah (hai Muhammad kepada mereka), kisah Musa di dalam Al Kitab (Al Quran) ini. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang dipilih dan seorang rasul dan nabi.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Muhammad, ceritakan pula kepada mereka kisah Mûsâ yang ada di dalam al-Qur'ân. Sesungguhnya ia adalah orang yang tulus jiwa, hati dan jasmaninya kepada Allah. Dan ia telah dipilih sebagai nabi dan rasul.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah kisah Musa di dalam Alquran ini. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang mukhlis) dapat dibaca Mukhlishan dan Mukhlashan, artinya seorang yang ikhlas di dalam beribadah dan Allah membersihkan dirinya dari hal-hal yang kotor (di samping ia adalah seorang rasul dan nabi).
t_id.iwq: Dan ceritakanlah (hai Muhammad kepada mereka), kisah Musa di dalam Al Kitab (Al Al-Quran) ini. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang dipilih dan seorang rasul dan nabi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «واذكر في الكتاب موسى إنه كان مخلصا» بكسر اللام وفتحها من أخلص في عبادته وخلصه الله من الدنس «وكان رسولاً نبيا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ مُوسَىٰٓۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ مُخۡلَصٗا وَكَانَ رَسُولٗا نَّبِيّٗا (مُخْلَصًا: مُصْطَفًى مُخْتَارًا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah kisah Musa di dalam Alquran ini. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang mukhlis) dapat dibaca Mukhlishan dan Mukhlashan, artinya seorang yang ikhlas di dalam beribadah dan Allah membersihkan dirinya dari hal-hal yang kotor (di samping ia adalah seorang rasul dan nabi)

ar: وَنَـٰدَیۡنَـٰهُ مِن جَانِبِ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلۡأَیۡمَنِ وَقَرَّبۡنَـٰهُ نَجِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَنَادَيْنَاهُ مِنْ جَانِبِ الطُّورِ الْأَيْمَنِ وَقَرَّبْنَاهُ نَجِيًّا
transliteration: wanādaynāhu min jānibi l-ṭūri l-aymani waqarrabnāhu najiyyan

t.sahih-int: And We called him from the side of the mount at [his] right and brought him near, confiding [to him]
t.asad: And [remember how] We called upon him from the right-hand slope of Mount Sinai and drew him near [unto Us] in mystic communion
t.yusuf-ali: And we called him from the right side of Mount (Sinai), and made him draw near to Us, for mystic (converse)
t.pickthall: We called him from the right slope of the Mount, and brought him nigh in communion
t.arberry: We called to him from the right side Of the Mount, and We brought him near in communion
t.hilali-khan: And We called him from the right side of the Mount, and made him draw near to Us for a talk with him [Musa (Moses]
t.itani: And We called him from the right side of the Mount, and brought him near in communion.
t.mubarakpuri: And We called him from the right side of the Mount (Tur), and made him draw near to Us for a talk with him.
t.qarib: We called out to him from the right hand side of the mount and We brought him near to Us in close communication
t.wahiduddin: We called out to him from the right side of the mount and made him draw near to be in close communion with Us
t.shakir: And We called to him from the blessed side of the mountain, and We made him draw nigh, holding communion (with Us)
t.haleem: We called to him from the right-hand side of the mountain and brought him close to Us in secret communion
t.maariful: And We called him from the right side of the mount Tūr (Sinai), and We brought him close to communicate in secret
t.khattab: We called him from the right side of Mount Ṭûr, and drew him near, speaking ˹with him˺ directly
t.ghali: And We called out to him from the right side of At-Tur (The Mount) and We brought him near in private conference
t.irving: We called out to him from the right side of the Mountain, and brought him close to confide in
t.iwq: And We called him from the side of the mount at his right and brought him near, confiding to him

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami telah memanggilnya dari sebelah kanan gunung Thur dan Kami telah mendekatkannya kepada Kami di waktu dia munajat (kepada Kami).
t_id.sabiq: Mûsâ Kami beri kemuliaan dan Kami panggil di gunung Thûr. Ia pun mendengar panggilan Tuhannya dari arah kanan. Sebagai pemuliaan, ia Kami dekatkan dan Kami pilih untuk bermunajat kepada Kami.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah memanggilnya) melalui firman-Nya, "Hai Musa! Sesungguhnya Aku adalah Allah..." (Q.S. Al-Qashash, 30). (dari arah Thur) nama sebuah bukit (sebelah kanan) yakni dari sebelah kanan Nabi Musa ketika ia baru datang dari negeri Madyan (dan Kami telah mendekatkannya kepada Kami waktu dia munajat) bermunajat, yaitu Allah memperdengarkan Kalam-Nya kepadanya.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami telah memanggilnya dari sebelah kanan gunung Thur dan Kami telah mendekatkannya kepada Kami di waktu dia munajat (kepada Kami).

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: (وناديناه) بقول "" يا موسى إني أنا الله "" (من جانب الطور) اسم جبل (الأيمن) أي الذي يلي يمين موسى حين أقبل من مدين (وقربناه نجيا) مناجيا بأن أسمعه الله تعالى كلامه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَنَٰدَيۡنَٰهُ مِن جَانِبِ ٱلطُّورِ ٱلۡأَيۡمَنِ وَقَرَّبۡنَٰهُ نَجِيّٗا (الطُّورِ: جَبَلٌ بِسَيْنَاءَ, نَجِيًّا: مُنَاجِيًا لَنَا)
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah memanggilnya) melalui firman-Nya, "Hai Musa! Sesungguhnya Aku adalah Allah..." (Q.S. Al-Qashash, 30). (dari arah Thur) nama sebuah bukit (sebelah kanan) yakni dari sebelah kanan Nabi Musa ketika ia baru datang dari negeri Madyan (dan Kami telah mendekatkannya kepada Kami waktu dia munajat) bermunajat, yaitu Allah memperdengarkan Kalam-Nya kepadanya

ar: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥ مِن رَّحۡمَتِنَاۤ أَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ نَبِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُ مِنْ رَحْمَتِنَا أَخَاهُ هَارُونَ نَبِيًّا
transliteration: wawahabnā lahu min raḥmatinā akhāhu hārūna nabiyyan

t.sahih-int: And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron as a prophet
t.asad: and [how], out of Our grace, We granted unto him his brother Aaron, to be a prophet [by his side]
t.yusuf-ali: And, out of Our Mercy, We gave him his brother Aaron, (also) a prophet
t.pickthall: And We bestowed upon him of Our mercy his brother Aaron, a prophet (likewise)
t.arberry: And We gave him his brother Aaron, of Our mercy, a Prophet
t.hilali-khan: And We bestowed on him his brother Harun (Aaron), (also) a Prophet, out of Our Mercy
t.itani: And We granted him, out of Our mercy, his brother Aaron, a prophet.
t.mubarakpuri: And We granted him his brother Harun, (also) a Prophet, out of Our mercy.
t.qarib: and out of Our mercy We gave him his brother Aaron as a prophet
t.wahiduddin: and We gave him as his helper, out of Our mercy, his brother Aaron, having made him a prophet
t.shakir: And We gave to him out of Our mercy his brother Haroun a prophet
t.haleem: out of Our grace We granted him his brother Aaron as a prophet
t.maariful: And, out of Our mercy, We granted him his brother Hārūn as a prophet
t.khattab: And We appointed for him—out of Our grace—his brother, Aaron, as a prophet
t.ghali: And We bestowed upon him of Our mercy his brother Har?n, (Aaron) a Prophet
t.irving: We bestowed his brother Aaron on him as a prophet through Our mercy
t.iwq: And We gave him out of Our mercy his brother Aaron as a prophet

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami telah menganugerahkan kepadanya sebagian rahmat Kami, yaitu saudaranya, Harun menjadi seorang nabi.
t_id.sabiq: Kami berikan kepadanya sebagian dari rahmat dan nikmat Kami. Selain itu, saudara Mûsâ, Hârûn, juga Kami jadikan nabi untuk membantu dalam menyampaikan risalah.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah menganugerahkan kepadanya sebagian rahmat Kami) sebagian dari nikmat Kami (yaitu saudaranya, Harun) Lafal Haruna menjadi Badal atau Athaf Bayan (menjadi seorang nabi) lafal Nabiyyan ini menjadi Hal atau kata keterangan yang dimaksud daripada pemberian itu; hal ini merupakan pengabulan dari doa Nabi Musa sendiri yang meminta kepada Allah, supaya Dia mengangkat saudara tuanya menjadi rasul pula.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami telah menganugerahkan kepadanya sebagian rahmat Kami, yaitu saudaranya, Harun menjadi seorang nabi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ووهينا له من رحمتنا» نعمتنا «أخاه هارون» بدل أو عطف بيان «نبيا» حال هي المقصودة بالهبة إجابة لسؤاله أن يرسل أخاه معه وكان أسنَّ منه
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَوَهَبۡنَا لَهُۥ مِن رَّحۡمَتِنَآ أَخَاهُ هَٰرُونَ نَبِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah menganugerahkan kepadanya sebagian rahmat Kami) sebagian dari nikmat Kami (yaitu saudaranya, Harun) Lafal Haruna menjadi Badal atau Athaf Bayan (menjadi seorang nabi) lafal Nabiyyan ini menjadi Hal atau kata keterangan yang dimaksud daripada pemberian itu; hal ini merupakan pengabulan dari doa Nabi Musa sendiri yang meminta kepada Allah, supaya Dia mengangkat saudara tuanya menjadi rasul pula

ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِی ٱلۡكِتَـٰبِ إِسۡمَـٰعِیلَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صَادِقَ ٱلۡوَعۡدِ وَكَانَ رَسُولࣰا نَّبِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ صَادِقَ الْوَعْدِ وَكَانَ رَسُولًا نَبِيًّا
transliteration: wa-udh'kur fī l-kitābi is'māʿīla innahu kāna ṣādiqa l-waʿdi wakāna rasūlan nabiyyan

t.sahih-int: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet
t.asad: AND CALL to mind, through this divine writ, Ishmael. Behold, he was always true to his promise, and was an apostle [of God], a prophet
t.yusuf-ali: Also mention in the Book (the story of) Isma'il: He was (strictly) true to what he promised, and he was a messenger (and) a prophet
t.pickthall: And make mention in the Scripture of Ishmael. Lo! he was a keeper of his promise, and he was a messenger (of Allah), a prophet
t.arberry: And mention in the Book Ishmael; he was true to his promise, and he was a Messenger, a Prophet
t.hilali-khan: And mention in the Book (the Quran) Isma'il (Ishmael). Verily! He was true to what he promised, and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet
t.itani: And mention in the Scripture Ishmael. He was true to his promise, and was a messenger, a prophet.
t.mubarakpuri: And mention in the Book, Isma`il. Verily, he was true to what he promised, and he was a Messenger, (and) a Prophet.
t.qarib: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. He was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet
t.wahiduddin: Tell also of Ishmael in the Book. He was true to his promise and was a messenger and a prophet
t.shakir: And mention Ismail in the Book; surely he was truthful in (his) promise, and he was an apostle, a prophet
t.haleem: Mention too, in the Quran, the story of Ishmael. He was true to his promise, a messenger and a prophet
t.maariful: And mention in the Book (the story of) Ismā‘īl. He was indeed true to his promise, and was a messenger, a prophet
t.khattab: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Ishmael. He was truly a man of his word, and was a messenger and a prophet
t.ghali: And mention in the Book Shuaayb; (Ishmael) surely he was sincerely (true) to his promise, and he was a Messenger, a Prophet
t.irving: Mention Ishmael in the Book. He kept true to the Promise, and was a messenger, a prophet
t.iwq: And mention in the Book, Ishmael. Indeed, he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger and a prophet

t_id.kemenag: Dan ceritakanlah (hai Muhammad kepada mereka) kisah Ismail (yang tersebut) di dalam Al Quran. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang benar janjinya, dan dia adalah seorang rasul dan nabi.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Muhammad, ceritakan juga kepada mereka kisah Ismâ'îl yang ada di dalam al-Qur'ân. Sesungguhnya ia adalah orang yang menepati janji. Ismâ'îl telah berjanji kepada bapaknya untuk sabar dan tabah atas penyembelihan dirinya, dan ia menepatinya. Lalu ia diganti, dengan seekor kambing, dan dimuliakan dengan membawa misi kerasulan dan kenabian.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah kisah Ismail di dalam Alquran. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang benar janjinya) sekali-kali ia tidak menjanjikan sesuatu melainkan ia memenuhinya. Disebutkan bahwa ia pernah menunggu seseorang yang telah berjanji kepadanya, selama tiga hari atau satu tahun, sehingga orang yang berjanji itu datang kepadanya di tempat yang dijanjikan itu, dan ternyata Nabi Ismail masih menunggu di tempat itu (dan dia adalah seorang rasul) untuk kabilah Jurhum (dan nabi).
t_id.iwq: Dan ceritakanlah (hai Muhammad kepada mereka) kisah Ismail (yang tersebut) di dalam Al Al-Quran. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang benar janjinya, dan dia adalah seorang rasul dan nabi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «واذكر في الكتاب إسماعيل إنه كان صادق الوعد» لم يعد شيئا إلا وفى به وانتظر من وعده ثلاثة أيام أيام أو حولاً حتى رجع إليه في مكانه «وكان رسولاً» إلى جُرهم «نبيا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ إِسۡمَٰعِيلَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صَادِقَ ٱلۡوَعۡدِ وَكَانَ رَسُولٗا نَّبِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah kisah Ismail di dalam Alquran. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang benar janjinya) sekali-kali ia tidak menjanjikan sesuatu melainkan ia memenuhinya. Disebutkan bahwa ia pernah menunggu seseorang yang telah berjanji kepadanya, selama tiga hari atau satu tahun, sehingga orang yang berjanji itu datang kepadanya di tempat yang dijanjikan itu, dan ternyata Nabi Ismail masih menunggu di tempat itu (dan dia adalah seorang rasul) untuk kabilah Jurhum (dan nabi)

ar: وَكَانَ یَأۡمُرُ أَهۡلَهُۥ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ وَكَانَ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦ مَرۡضِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَكَانَ يَأْمُرُ أَهْلَهُ بِالصَّلَاةِ وَالزَّكَاةِ وَكَانَ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِ مَرْضِيًّا
transliteration: wakāna yamuru ahlahu bil-ṣalati wal-zakati wakāna ʿinda rabbihi marḍiyyan

t.sahih-int: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakah and was to his Lord pleasing
t.asad: who used to enjoin upon his people prayer and charity, and found favour in his Sustainer's sight
t.yusuf-ali: He used to enjoin on his people Prayer and Charity, and he was most acceptable in the sight of his Lord
t.pickthall: He enjoined upon his people worship and almsgiving, and was acceptable in the sight of his Lord
t.arberry: He bade his people to pray and to give the alms, and he was pleasing to his Lord
t.hilali-khan: And he used to enjoin on his family and his people As-Salat (the prayers) and the Zakat, and his Lord was pleased with him
t.itani: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and charity, and he was pleasing to his Lord.
t.mubarakpuri: And he would enjoin on his family and his people the Salah and the Zakah, and his Lord was pleased with him.
t.qarib: He used to order his people to pray and to give the purifying alms and he found favor with His Lord
t.wahiduddin: He exhorted his people to prayer and almsgiving, and his Lord was pleased with him
t.shakir: And he enjoined on his family prayer and almsgiving, and was one in whom his Lord was well pleased
t.haleem: He commanded his household to pray and give alms, and his Lord was well pleased with him
t.maariful: He used to enjoin Salāh and Zakāh upon his family, and was favourite to his Lord
t.khattab: He used to urge his people to pray and give alms-tax. And his Lord was well pleased with him
t.ghali: And he used to command his family to (keep up) the prayer and (to give) Zakat; (The poordues) and he was most satisfied in the Providence of his Lord
t.irving: He used to order his people to pray and [pay] the welfare tax; he was approved by his Lord
t.iwq: And he used to enjoin on his people prayer and zakah and was to his Lord pleasing

t_id.kemenag: Dan ia menyuruh ahlinya untuk bersembahyang dan menunaikan zakat, dan ia adalah seorang yang diridhai di sisi Tuhannya.
t_id.sabiq: Ia menyuruh keluarganya untuk mendirikan salat dan menunaikan zakat. Dan ia adalah seorang yang mendapatkan tempat yang mulia berupa keridaan Tuhannya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ia menyuruh ahlinya) yakni kaumnya (untuk salat dan menunaikan zakat dan ia adalah seorang yang diridai di sisi Rabbnya) lafal Mardhiyyan asalnya Mardhuwwun, kedua huruf Wawunya diganti menjadi Ya. Selanjutnya harakat Dhammah Dhadhnya diganti menjadi Kasrah, akhirnya jadi Mardhiyyun, oleh karena kedudukannya menjadi Khabar Kaana maka bacaannya menjadi Mardhiyyan.
t_id.iwq: Dan ia menyuruh ahlinya untuk bersembahyang dan menunaikan zakat, dan ia adalah seorang yang diridhai di sisi Tuhannya.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «وكان يأمر أهله» أي قومه «بالصلاة والزكاة وكان عند ربه مرضيا» أصله مرضوو قبلت الواوان ياءين والضمة كسرة
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَكَانَ يَأۡمُرُ أَهۡلَهُۥ بِٱلصَّلَوٰةِ وَٱلزَّكَوٰةِ وَكَانَ عِندَ رَبِّهِۦ مَرۡضِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ia menyuruh ahlinya) yakni kaumnya (untuk salat dan menunaikan zakat dan ia adalah seorang yang diridai di sisi Rabbnya) lafal Mardhiyyan asalnya Mardhuwwun, kedua huruf Wawunya diganti menjadi Ya. Selanjutnya harakat Dhammah Dhadhnya diganti menjadi Kasrah, akhirnya jadi Mardhiyyun, oleh karena kedudukannya menjadi Khabar Kaana maka bacaannya menjadi Mardhiyyan

ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِی ٱلۡكِتَـٰبِ إِدۡرِیسَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّیقࣰا نَّبِیࣰّا
ar_simple: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ إِدْرِيسَ ۚ إِنَّهُ كَانَ صِدِّيقًا نَبِيًّا
transliteration: wa-udh'kur fī l-kitābi id'rīsa innahu kāna ṣiddīqan nabiyyan

t.sahih-int: And mention in the Book, Idrees. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet
t.asad: AND CALL to mind, through this divine writ, Idris. Behold, he was a man of truth, a prophet
t.yusuf-ali: Also mention in the Book the case of Idris: He was a man of truth (and sincerity), (and) a prophet
t.pickthall: And make mention in the Scripture of Idris. Lo! he was a saint, a prophet
t.arberry: And mention in the Book Idris; he was a true man, a Prophet
t.hilali-khan: And mention in the Book (the Quran) Idris (Enoch). Verily! He was a man of truth, (and) a Prophet
t.itani: And mention in the Scripture Enoch. He was a man of truth, a prophet.
t.mubarakpuri: And mention in the Book, Idris. Verily, he was a man of truth, (and) a Prophet.
t.qarib: And mention in the Book, Enoch (Idris) He was a man of truth and a prophet
t.wahiduddin: Tell also of Idris in the Book. He was a man of truth and a prophet
t.shakir: And mention Idris in the Book; surely he was a truthful man, a prophet
t.haleem: Mention too, in the Quran, the story of Idris. He was a man of truth, a prophet
t.maariful: And mention in the Book (the story of) Idrīs. He was indeed a Siddīq (man of truth), a prophet
t.khattab: And mention in the Book ˹O Prophet, the story of˺ Enoch. He was surely a man of truth and a prophet
t.ghali: And mention in the Book Idris; surely he was most sincere, a Prophet
t.irving: Mention Idifs in the Book. He was a truthful prophet
t.iwq: And mention in the Book, Idrees. Indeed, he was a man of truth and a prophet

t_id.kemenag: Dan ceritakanlah (hai Muhammad kepada mereka, kisah) Idris (yang tersebut) di dalam Al Quran. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan dan seorang nabi.
t_id.sabiq: Wahai Muhammad, ceritakan juga kepada mereka kisah Idrîs yang ada di dalam al-Qur'ân. Sesungguhnya Idrîs adalah seorang yang benar perkataan dan perbuatannya. Allah telah memberikan anugerah kenabian kepadanya.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah kisah Idris di dalam Alquran) Nabi Idris adalah buyut Nabi Ibrahim atau ayah kakek Nabi Nuh (Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan dan seorang nabi).
t_id.iwq: Dan ceritakanlah (hai Muhammad kepada mereka, kisah) Idris (yang tersebut) di dalam Al Al-Quran. Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan dan seorang nabi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «واذكر في الكتاب إدريس» هو جدّ أبي نوح «إنه كان صديقا نبيا»
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَٱذۡكُرۡ فِي ٱلۡكِتَٰبِ إِدۡرِيسَۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ صِدِّيقٗا نَّبِيّٗا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan ceritakanlah kisah Idris di dalam Alquran) Nabi Idris adalah buyut Nabi Ibrahim atau ayah kakek Nabi Nuh (Sesungguhnya ia adalah seorang yang sangat membenarkan dan seorang nabi)

ar: وَرَفَعۡنَـٰهُ مَكَانًا عَلِیًّا
ar_simple: وَرَفَعْنَاهُ مَكَانًا عَلِيًّا
transliteration: warafaʿnāhu makānan ʿaliyyan

t.sahih-int: And We raised him to a high station
t.asad: whom We exalted onto a lofty station
t.yusuf-ali: And We raised him to a lofty station
t.pickthall: And We raised him to high station
t.arberry: We raised him up to a high place
t.hilali-khan: And We raised him to a high station
t.itani: And We raised him to a high position.
t.mubarakpuri: And We raised him to a high station.
t.qarib: and We raised him to a high position
t.wahiduddin: We raised him to a high position
t.shakir: And We raised him high in Heaven
t.haleem: We raised him to a high position
t.maariful: We raised him to a high place
t.khattab: And We elevated him to an honourable status
t.ghali: And We raised him to a most exalted place
t.irving: We raised him to a lofty place
t.iwq: And We raised him to a high station

t_id.kemenag: Dan Kami telah mengangkatnya ke martabat yang tinggi.
t_id.sabiq: Dengan kenabian itu, ia diangkat ke derajat yang tinggi.
t_id.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah mengangkatnya ke martabat yang tinggi) ia masih tetap hidup sampai sekarang bertempat di langit keempat atau keenam, atau ketujuh atau berada di dalam surga. Ia dimasukkan ke dalam surga setelah terlebih dahulu mencicipi rasanya mati lalu dihidupkan kembali, setelah itu ia tidak mau keluar lagi dari dalam surga.
t_id.iwq: Dan Kami telah mengangkatnya ke martabat yang tinggi.

tafsir.jalalayn-ar: «ورفعناه مكانا عليا» هو حي في السماء الرابعة أو السادسة أو السابعة أو في الجنة أدخلها بعد أن أذيق الموت وأحيي ولم يخرج منها
tafsir.siraj-ar: وَرَفَعۡنَٰهُ مَكَانًا عَلِيًّا
tafsir.jalalayn-id: (Dan Kami telah mengangkatnya ke martabat yang tinggi) ia masih tetap hidup sampai sekarang bertempat di langit keempat atau keenam, atau ketujuh atau berada di dalam surga. Ia dimasukkan ke dalam surga setelah terlebih dahulu mencicipi rasanya mati lalu dihidupkan kembali, setelah itu ia tidak mau keluar lagi dari dalam surga

ar: أُو۟لَـٰۤئِكَ ٱلَّذِینَ أَنۡعَمَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَیۡهِم مِّنَ ٱلنَّبِیِّـۧنَ مِن ذُرِّیَّةِ ءَادَمَ وَمِمَّنۡ حَمَلۡنَا مَعَ نُوحࣲ وَمِن ذُرِّیَّةِ إِبۡرَ ٰهِیمَ وَإِسۡرَ ٰۤءِیلَ وَمِمَّنۡ هَدَیۡنَا وَٱجۡتَبَیۡنَاۤۚ إِذَا تُتۡلَىٰ عَلَیۡهِمۡ ءَایَـٰتُ ٱلرَّحۡمَـٰنِ خَ̅رُّ̅و̅ا۟̅ ̅سُ̅جَّ̅دࣰ̅ا̅ وَبُكِیࣰّا ۩
ar_simple: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ آدَمَ وَمِمَّنْ حَمَلْنَا مَعَ نُوحٍ وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْرَائِيلَ وَمِمَّنْ هَدَيْنَا وَاجْتَبَيْنَا ۚ إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُ الرَّحْمَٰنِ خَرُّوا سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا ۩
transliteration: ulāika alladhīna anʿama l-lahu ʿalayhim mina l-nabiyīna min dhurriyyati ādama wamimman ḥamalnā maʿa nūḥin wamin dhurriyyati ib'rāhīma wa-is'rāīla wamimman hadaynā wa-ij'tabaynā idhā tut'lā ʿalayhim āyātu l-raḥmāni kharrū sujjadan wabukiyyan

t.sahih-int: Those were the ones upon whom Allah bestowed favor from among the prophets of the descendants of Adam and of those We carried [in the ship] with Noah, and of the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose. When the verses of the Most Merciful were recited to them, they fell in prostration and weeping
t.asad: THESE WERE some of the prophets upon whom God bestowed His blessings - [prophets] of the seed of Adam and of those whom We caused to be borne [in the ark] with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel and [all of them were] among those whom We had guided and elected; [and] whenever the messages of the Most Gracious were conveyed unto them, they would fall down [before Him], prostrating themselves and weeping
t.yusuf-ali: Those were some of the prophets on whom Allah did bestow His Grace,- of the posterity of Adam, and of those who We carried (in the Ark) with Noah, and of the posterity of Abraham and Israel of those whom We guided and chose. Whenever the Signs of (Allah) Most Gracious were rehearsed to them, they would fall down in prostrate adoration and in tears
t.pickthall: These are they unto whom Allah showed favour from among the prophets, of the seed of Adam and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the revelations of the Beneficent were recited unto them, they fell down, adoring and weeping
t.arberry: These are they whom God has blessed among the Prophets of the seed of Adam, and of those We bore with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and of those We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-merciful were recited to them, they fell down prostrate, weeping
t.hilali-khan: Those were they unto whom Allah bestowed His Grace from among the Prophets, of the offspring of Adam, and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh (Noah), and of the offspring of Ibrahim (Abraham) and Israel and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the Verses of the Most Beneficent (Allah) were recited unto them, they fell down prostrating and weeping
t.itani: These are some of the prophets God has blessed, from the descendants of Adam, and from those We carried with Noah, and from the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and from those We guided and selected. Whenever the revelations of the Most Gracious are recited to them, they would fall down, prostrating and weeping.
t.mubarakpuri: Those were they unto whom Allah bestowed His grace from among the Prophets, of the offspring of Adam, and of those whom We carried (in the ship) with Nuh, and of the offspring of Ibrahim and Israel, and from among those whom We guided and chose. When the Ayat of the Most Gracious were recited unto them, they fell down prostrate and weeping.
t.qarib: These are some of the prophets whom God has blessed- from among the descendants of Adam, and those We carried with Noah, and the descendants of Abraham and Israel, and those whom We guided and chose. When the verses of the Merciful were recited to them, they fell down prostrating and weeping
t.wahiduddin: These are the ones whom God has favoured: the prophets from among the descendants of Adam and of those whom We carried in the Ark with Noah; the descendants of Abraham, of Israel, and of those whom We have guided and chosen. For when the revelations of the Merciful were recited to them, they fell down, prostrating themselves and weeping
t.shakir: These are they on whom Allah bestowed favors, from among the prophets of the seed of Adam, and of those whom We carried with Nuh, and of the seed of Ibrahim and Israel, and of those whom We guided and chose; when the communications of the Beneficent Allah were recited to them, they fell down making obeisance and weeping
